а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ц-а§Ѓа§Ња§Є-а§Еа§®а•На§§а§ња§Ѓа§§а•На§∞а§ѓа§§а§ња§•а§њ-а§µа•На§∞а§§-а§Жа§∞а§Ѓа•На§≠а§Г

Observed on ≈Ъukla-Tray≈Нda≈ЫƒЂ tithi of Vai≈ЫƒБkhaбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

Last three days of vai≈ЫƒБkha-mƒБsaбЄ•. anantaphaladam! Perform Shiva/Vishnu Puja, Bhagavata Parayanam, BhagavadGita Parayanam, Vishnu Sahasranama Parayanam, dƒБna of ap≈Ђpa, pƒБyasa etc.

а§ѓа§Г а§Ѓа§Ња§Іа§µа•З а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•З, а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•Ла§¶а§ґа•Аа§Ъа§§а•Ба§∞а•На§¶а§ґа•Аа§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§ња§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•Б а§§а§ња§•а§ња§Ја•Б а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§®а§Ња§¶а§ња§Ха§В а§Ха§∞а•Ла§§а§њ
а§Є а§®а§ња§ґа•На§Ъа§ѓа•За§® а§Ѓа•Ла§Ха•На§Ја§В а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§™а•На§®а•Ба§ѓа§Ња§§а•На•§ а§ѓа§•а§Њ а§™а•Ба§∞а§Њ а§Іа§®а§ґа§∞а•На§Ѓа§Њ а§ђа•На§∞а§Ња§єа•На§Ѓа§£а§Г а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа§Г а§™а•На§∞а•За§§а§Ња§ґа•На§Ъ
а§Ѓа•Ба§Ха•На§§а§ња§В а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§™а•Ба§∞а§ња§§а§ња•§ а§Еа§§а•На§∞ а§™а•На§∞а§Єа§ња§¶а•На§Іа§Њ а§Ха§•а§Њ а§Єа§™а•На§§а§¶а§ґа§Ња§Іа•На§ѓа§Ња§ѓа§Ња§§а•На§Ѓа§Ха§™а§Ња§¶а•На§Ѓа•Ла§Ха•На§§а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца§Ѓа§Ња§єа§Ња§§а•На§Ѓа•На§ѓа•За•§
а§™а•Ба§∞а§Њ а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§®а§Њ а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•Ла§¶а§ґа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§єа§ња§∞а§£а•На§ѓа§Ња§Ха•На§Ја§Г, а§Ъа§§а•Ба§∞а•На§¶а§ґа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§Ѓа§Іа•Ба§ґа•На§Ъ а§Ша§Ња§§а§ња§§а§Г,
а§™а•Ма§∞а•На§£а§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§≠а•Ва§Ѓа•На§ѓа•Ба§¶а•На§Іа§Ња§∞а§ґа•На§Ъ а§Ха•Га§§а§Г, а§§а§§а§Г а§™а•На§∞а§≠а•Га§§а§њ а§§а§Ња§Єа•На§§а§ња§•а§ѓа§Г а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§Њ а§Ьа§Ња§§а§Ња§Г!
а§ґа•На§∞а•Ба§§а§¶а•За§µ а§Йа§µа§Ња§Ъ—
а§ѓа§Ња§Єа•На§§а§ња§Єа•На§∞а§Єа•На§§а§ња§•а§ѓа§Г а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§Њ а§Еа§®а•На§§а§ња§Ѓа§Ња§Г а§ґа•Ба§Ха•На§≤а§™а§Ха•На§Ја§Ха•За•§
а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§њ а§∞а§Ња§Ьа•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§ња§Ѓа§Ња§®а•На§§а§Ња§Г а§ґа•Ба§≠а§Ња§µа§єа§Ња§Га••а•Іа••
а§Еа§®а•На§§а•На§ѓа§Ња§Г а§™а•Ба§Ја•На§Ха§∞а§ња§£а•Аа§Єа§Ва§Ьа•На§Юа§Ња§Г а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§™а§Ња§™а§Ха•На§Ја§ѓа§Ња§µа§єа§Ња§Га•§
а§Ѓа§Ња§Іа§µа•З а§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§њ а§ѓа§Г а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§В а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§®а§В а§Ха§∞а•На§§а•Ба§В а§® а§Ъ а§Ха•На§Ја§Ѓа§Га••а•®а••
а§§а§ња§•а§ња§Ја•На§µа•За§§а§Ња§Єа•Б а§Є а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§§а•На§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§Ѓа•За§µ а§Ђа§≤а§В а§≤а§≠а•За§§а•На•§
а§Єа§∞а•На§µа•З а§¶а•За§µа§Ња§Єа•На§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•Ла§¶а§ґа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§Єа•На§•а§ња§§а•На§µа§Њ а§Ьа§®а•На§§а•Ва§®а•На§™а•Ба§®а§®а•На§§а§њ а§єа§ња••а•©а••
а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§Г а§™а§∞а•На§µа§§а•Аа§∞а•На§•а•Иа§ґа•На§Ъ а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§®а§Њ а§Єа§є а§Єа§Ва§Єа•На§•а§ња§§а§Ња§Га•§
а§Ъа§§а•Ба§∞а•На§¶а§ґа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§Єа§ѓа§Ьа•На§Юа§Ња§ґа•На§Ъ а§¶а•За§µа§Њ а§Па§§а§Ња§®а•На§™а•Ба§®а§®а•На§§а§њ а§єа§ња••а•™а••
а§ђа•На§∞а§єа•На§Ѓа§Ша•На§®а§В а§µа§Њ а§Єа•Ба§∞а§Ња§™а§В а§µа§Њ а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§Ња§®а•За§§а§Ња§®а•На§™а•Ба§®а§®а•На§§а§њ а§єа§ња•§
а§Па§Ха§Ња§¶а§ґа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§™а•Ба§∞а§Њ а§Ьа§Ьа•На§Юа•З а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца•На§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§Ѓа•Га§§а§В а§ґа•Ба§≠а§Ѓа•На••а•Ђа••
а§¶а•На§µа§Ња§¶а§ґа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§™а§Ња§≤а§ња§§а§В а§§а§Ъа•На§Ъ а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§®а§Њ а§™а•На§∞а§≠а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§®а§Ња•§
а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•Ла§¶а§ґа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§Єа•Ба§Іа§Ња§В а§¶а•За§µа§Ња§®а•На§™а§Ња§ѓа§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§Ња§Є а§µа•И а§єа§∞а§ња§Га••а•ђа••
а§Ьа§Ша§Ња§® а§Ъ а§Ъа§§а•Ба§∞а•На§¶а§ґа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§¶а•Иа§§а•На§ѓа§Ња§®а•На§¶а•За§µа§µа§ња§∞а•Ла§Іа§ња§®а§Га•§
а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§В а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§¶а•За§µа§Ња§®а§Ња§В а§Єа§Ња§Ѓа•На§∞а§Ња§Ьа•На§ѓа§Ња§ља§ља§™а•На§§а§ња§∞а•На§ђа§≠а•Ва§µ а§єа••а•≠а••
а§§а§§а•Л а§¶а•За§µа§Ња§Г а§Єа•Ба§Єа§®а•На§§а•Ба§Ја•На§Яа§Њ а§Па§§а§Ња§Єа§Ња§В а§Ъ а§µа§∞а§В а§¶а§¶а•Ба§Га•§
а§§а§ња§Єа•Га§£а§Ња§В а§Ъ а§§а§ња§•а•Аа§®а§Ња§В а§µа•И а§™а•На§∞а•Аа§§а•На§ѓа•Ла§§а•На§Ђа•Ба§≤а•На§≤а§µа§ња§≤а•Ла§Ъа§®а§Ња§Га••а•Ѓа••
а§Па§§а§Њ а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§Єа•На§ѓ а§§а§ња§Єа•На§∞а§ґа•На§Ъ а§§а§ња§•а§ѓа§Г а§ґа•Ба§≠а§Ња§Га•§
а§™а•Ба§§а•На§∞а§™а•Ма§§а•На§∞а§Ња§¶а§ња§Ђа§≤а§¶а§Њ а§®а§∞а§Ња§£а§Ња§В а§™а§Ња§™а§єа§Ња§®а§ња§¶а§Ња§Га••а•ѓа••
а§ѓа•Ла§ља§Єа•На§Ѓа§ња§®а•На§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•З а§Ъ а§Єа§Ѓа•На§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а•З а§® а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§§а•Л а§Ѓа§®а•Ба§Ьа§Ња§Іа§Ѓа§Га•§
а§§а§ња§•а§ња§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•З а§§а•Б а§Є а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§§а•На§µа§Њ а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§Ѓа•За§µ а§Ђа§≤а§В а§≤а§≠а•За§§а•На••а•Іа•¶а••
а§§а§ња§•а§ња§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За§™а•На§ѓа§Ха•Ба§∞а•На§µа§Ња§£а§Г а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§®а§¶а§Ња§®а§Ња§¶а§ња§Ха§В а§®а§∞а§Га•§
а§Ъа§Ња§£а•На§°а§Ња§≤а•Аа§В а§ѓа•Ла§®а§ња§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§Ња§¶а•На§ѓ а§™а§ґа•На§Ъа§Ња§¶а•На§∞а•Ма§∞а§µа§Ѓа§ґа•На§®а•Ба§§а•За••а•Іа•Іа••
а§Йа§Ја•На§£а•Ла§¶а§Ха•За§® а§ѓа§Г а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§§а§њ а§Ѓа§Ња§Іа§µа•З а§Ъ а§§а§ња§•а§ња§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§∞а•Ма§∞а§µа§В а§®а§∞а§Ха§В а§ѓа§Ња§§а§њ а§ѓа§Ња§µа§¶а§ња§Ва§¶а•На§∞а§Ња§ґа•На§Ъа§§а•Ба§∞а•На§¶а§ґа••а•Іа•®а••
а§™а§ња§§а•Да§®а•На§¶а•За§µа§Ња§®а•На§Єа§Ѓа•Ба§¶а•На§¶а§ња§ґа•На§ѓ а§¶а§Іа•На§ѓа§®а•На§®а§В а§® а§¶а§¶а§Ња§§а§њ а§ѓа§Га•§
а§™а•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ъа•Аа§В а§ѓа•Ла§®а§ња§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§Ња§¶а•На§ѓ а§§а§ња§Ја•На§†а§§а•На§ѓа§Ња§≠а•Ва§§а§Єа§Ѓа•На§™а•На§≤а§µа§Ѓа•На••а•Іа•©а••
а§™а•На§∞а§µа•Га§§а•На§§а§Ња§®а§Ња§В а§Ъ а§Ха§Ња§Ѓа§Ња§®а§Ња§В а§Ѓа§Ња§Іа§µа•З а§®а§ња§ѓа§Ѓа•З а§Ха•Га§§а•За•§
а§Еа§µа§ґа•На§ѓа§В а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§Єа§Ња§ѓа•Ба§Ьа•На§ѓа§В а§ѓа•Ба§Ьа•На§ѓа§§а•З а§®а§Ња§§а•На§∞ а§Єа§Ва§ґа§ѓа§Га••а•Іа•™а••
а§Жа§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§В а§®а§ња§ѓа§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§Ха•На§§а§Г а§Ха•Ба§∞а•На§ѓа§Ња§¶а•На§ѓа§¶а§њ а§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§§а•За§® а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§Ђа§≤а§В а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§™а•На§ѓ а§Ѓа•Ла§¶а§§а•З а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§Ѓа§®а•На§¶а§ња§∞а•За••а•Іа•Ђа••
а§ѓа•Л а§µа•И а§¶а•За§µа§Ња§®а•На§™а§ња§§а•Да§®а•На§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§В а§Ча•Ба§∞а•Ба§Ѓа•Ба§¶а•На§¶а§ња§ґа•На§ѓ а§Ѓа§Ња§®а§µа§Га•§
а§® а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§®а§Ња§¶а§њ а§Ха§∞а•Ла§§а•На§ѓа§¶а•На§Іа§Ња§ља§Ѓа•Ба§Ја•На§ѓ а§ґа§Ња§™а§™а•На§∞а§¶а§Њ а§µа§ѓа§Ѓа•На••а•Іа•ђа••
а§®а§ња§Га§Єа§®а•На§§а§Ња§®а•Л а§®а§ња§∞а§Ња§ѓа•Ба§ґа•На§Ъ а§®а§ња§Га§ґа•На§∞а•За§ѓа§Єа•На§Ха•Л а§≠а§µа•За§¶а§ња§§а§ња•§
а§За§§а§њ а§¶а•За§µа§Њ а§µа§∞а§В а§¶а§§а•На§§а•На§µа§Њ а§Єа•На§µа§Іа§Ња§Ѓа§Ња§®а§њ а§ѓа§ѓа•Ба§Г а§™а•Ба§∞а§Ња••а•Іа•≠а••
а§§а§Єа•На§Ѓа§Ња§§а•На§§а§ња§•а§ња§§а•На§∞а§ѓа§В а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§В а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§Ња§Ша•Ма§Ша§µа§ња§®а§Ња§ґа§®а§Ѓа•На•§
а§Еа§®а•На§§а•На§ѓа§В а§™а•Ба§Ја•На§Ха§∞а§ња§£а•Аа§Єа§Ва§Ьа•На§Юа§В а§™а•Ба§§а•На§∞а§™а•Ма§§а•На§∞а§µа§ња§µа§∞а•На§Іа§®а§Ѓа•На••а•Іа•Ѓа••
а§ѓа§Њ а§®а§Ња§∞а•А а§Єа•Ба§≠а§Ча§Ња§ља§ља§™а•Ва§™а§™а§Ња§ѓа§Єа§В а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§ња§Ѓа§Ња§¶а§ња§®а•За•§
а§ђа•На§∞а§Ња§єа•На§Ѓа§£а§Ња§ѓ а§Єа§Ха•Га§¶а•На§¶а§¶а•На§ѓа§Ња§§а•На§Ха•Аа§∞а•На§§а§ња§Ѓа§®а•На§§а§В а§Єа•Ба§§а§В а§≤а§≠а•За§§а•На••а•Іа•ѓа••
а§Ча•Аа§§а§Ња§™а§Ња§†а§В а§§а•Б а§ѓа§Г а§Ха•Ба§∞а•На§ѓа§Ња§¶а§®а•На§§а§ња§Ѓа•З а§Ъ а§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§¶а§ња§®а•За§¶а§ња§®а•За§ља§ґа•На§µа§Ѓа•За§Іа§Ња§®а§Ња§В а§Ђа§≤а§Ѓа•За§§а§њ а§® а§Єа§Ва§ґа§ѓа§Га••а•®а•¶а••
а§Єа§єа§Єа•На§∞а§®а§Ња§Ѓа§™а§†а§®а§В а§ѓа§Г а§Ха•Ба§∞а•На§ѓа•На§ѓа§Ња§Ъа•На§Ъ а§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§§а§Єа•На§ѓ а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§Ђа§≤а§В а§µа§Ха•На§§а•Ба§В а§Ха§Г а§ґа§Ха•На§§а•Л а§¶а§ња§µа§њ а§µа§Њ а§≠а•Ба§µа§ња••а•®а•Іа••
а§Єа§єа§Єа•На§∞а§®а§Ња§Ѓа§≠а§ња§∞а•На§¶а•За§µа§В а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§В а§Ѓа§Іа•Ба§Єа•Ва§¶а§®а§Ѓа•На•§
а§™а§ѓа§Єа§Њ а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§™а•На§ѓ а§µа•И а§ѓа§Ња§§а§њ а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§≤а•Ла§Ха§Ѓа§Ха§≤а•На§Ѓа§Ја§Ѓа•На••а•®а•®а••
а§Єа§Ѓа§Єа•На§§а§µа§ња§≠а§µа•Иа§∞а•На§ѓа§Єа•На§§а•Б а§™а•Ва§Ьа§ѓа•За§®а•На§Ѓа§Іа•Ба§Єа•Ва§¶а§®а§Ѓа•На•§
а§® а§§а§Єа•На§ѓ а§≤а•Ла§Ха§Ња§Г а§Ха•На§Ја•Аа§ѓа§®а•На§§а•З а§ѓа•Ба§Ч а§Ха§≤а•На§™а§Ња§¶а§њ а§µа•На§ѓа§§а•На§ѓа§ѓа•За••а•®а•©а••
а§Еа§Єа•На§®а§Ња§§а•На§µа§Њ а§Ъа§Ња§™а•На§ѓа§¶а§§а•На§§а•На§µа§Њ а§Ъ а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца§ґа•На§Ъ а§Ѓа§§а•Л а§ѓа§¶а§ња•§
а§Є а§ђа•На§∞а§єа•На§Ѓа§єа§Њ а§Ча•Ба§∞а•Ба§Ша•На§®а§ґа•На§Ъ а§™а§ња§§а•Да§£а§Ња§В а§Ша§Ња§§а§Ха§Єа•На§§а§•а§Ња••а•®а•™а••
а§ґа•На§≤а•Ла§Ха§Ња§∞а•На§Іа§В а§ґа•На§≤а•Ла§Ха§™а§Ња§¶а§В а§µа§Њ а§®а§ња§§а•На§ѓа§В а§≠а§Ња§Ча§µа§§а•Ла§¶а•На§≠а§µа§Ѓа•На•§
а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца•З а§Ъ а§™а§†а§®а•На§Ѓа§∞а•На§§а•На§ѓа•Л а§ђа•На§∞а§єа•На§Ѓа§§а•На§µа§В а§Ъа•Ла§™а§™а§¶а•На§ѓа§§а•За••а•®а•Ђа••
а§ѓа•Л а§µа•И а§≠а§Ња§Ча§µа§§а§В а§ґа§Ња§Єа•На§§а•На§∞а§В а§ґа•Га§£а•Ла§§а•На§ѓа•За§§а§¶а•На§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§® а§™а§Ња§™а•Иа§∞а•На§≤а§ња§™а•На§ѓа§§а•З а§Ха•На§µа§Ња§ља§™а§њ а§™а§¶а•На§Ѓа§™а§§а•На§∞а§Ѓа§ња§µа§Ња§Ва§≠а§Єа§Ња••а•®а•ђа••
а§¶а•За§µа§§а•На§µа§В а§Ѓа§®а•Ба§Ьа•Иа§Г а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§™а•На§§а§В а§Ха•Иа§ґа•На§Ъа§ња§§а•На§Єа§ња§¶а•На§Іа§§а•На§µа§Ѓа•За§µ а§Ъа•§
а§Ха•Иа§ґа•На§Ъа§ња§§а•На§™а•На§∞а§Ња§™а•На§§а•Л а§ђа•На§∞а§єа•На§Ѓа§≠а§Ња§µа•Л а§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа§®а§ња§Ја•За§µа§£а§Ња§§а•На••а•®а•≠а••
а§ђа•На§∞а§єа•На§Ѓа§Ьа•На§Юа§Ња§®а•За§® а§µа•И а§Ѓа•Ба§Ха•На§§а§ња§Г а§™а•На§∞а§ѓа§Ња§Ча§Ѓа§∞а§£а•За§® а§µа§Ња•§
а§Еа§•а§µа§Њ а§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§њ а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца•З а§®а§ња§ѓа§Ѓа•За§® а§Ьа§≤а§Ња§™а•На§≤а•Ба§§а•За§Га••а•®а•Ѓа••
а§®а•Аа§≤а§µа•Га§Ја§В а§Єа§Ѓа•Ба§§а•На§Єа•Га§Ьа•На§ѓ а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§Ъ а§Ьа§≤а§Ња§™а•На§≤а•Ба§§а•За§Га•§
а§Єа§Ѓа§Єа•На§§а§ђа§®а•На§Іа§®а§ња§∞а•На§Ѓа•Ба§Ха•На§§а§Г а§™а•Ба§Ѓа§Ња§®а•На§ѓа§Ња§§а§њ а§™а§∞а§В а§™а§¶а§Ѓа•На••а•®а•ѓа••
а§Ча§Ња§В а§Єа§µа§§а•На§Єа§Ња§В а§¶а•На§µа§ња§Ьа•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞а§Ња§ѓ а§Єа•Аа§¶а§§а•З а§Ъ а§Ха•Ба§Яа•Ба§Ѓа•На§ђа§ња§®а•За•§
а§За§єа§Ња§™а§Ѓа•Га§§а•На§ѓа•Ба§®а§ња§∞а•На§Ѓа•Ба§Ха•На§§а§Г а§™а§∞а§§а•На§∞ а§Ъ а§™а§∞а§В а§µа•На§∞а§Ьа•За§§а•На••а•©а•¶а••
а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§®а§¶а§Ња§®а§µа§ња§єа•Аа§®а§Єа•На§§а•Б а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца•А а§Ъа•Иа§µ а§ѓа•Л а§®а§ѓа•За§§а•На•§
а§ґа•На§µа§Ња§®а§ѓа•Ла§®а§ња§ґа§§а§В а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§™а•На§ѓ а§µа§ња§Ја•На§†а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§В а§Ьа§Ња§ѓа§§а•З а§Ха•Га§Ѓа§ња§Га••а•©а•Іа••
а§§а§ња§Єа•На§∞а§Г а§Ха•Ла§Яа•На§ѓа•Ла§ља§∞а•На§Іа§Ха•Ла§Яа§ња§ґа•На§Ъ а§§а•Аа§∞а•На§•а§Ња§®а§њ а§≠а•Ба§µа§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§Єа§Ѓа•На§≠а•Ва§ѓ а§Ѓа§®а•На§§а•На§∞а§ѓа§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа§Ха•На§∞а•Ба§Г а§™а§Ња§™а§Єа§Ща•На§Ша§Ња§§а§ґа§Ща•На§Ха§ња§§а§Ња§Га••а•©а•®а••
а§Ьа§®а§Њ а§Еа§Єа•На§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•Б а§™а§Ња§™а§ња§Ја•На§†а§Њ а§µа§ња§Єа•Га§Ьа§®а•На§§а§њ а§Єа•На§µа§Ха§В а§Ѓа§≤а§Ѓа•На•§
а§§а§¶а§Єа•На§Ѓа§Ња§Ха§В а§Ха§•а§В а§Ча§Ъа•На§Ыа•За§¶а§ња§§а§њ а§Ъа§ња§®а•На§§а§Ња§Єа§Ѓа§®а•На§µа§ња§§а§Ња§Га••а•©а•©а••
а§§а•Аа§∞а•На§•а§™а§Ња§¶а§В а§єа§∞а§ња§В а§Ьа§Ча•На§Ѓа•Ба§Г а§ґа§∞а§£а•На§ѓа§В а§ґа§∞а§£а§В а§µа§ња§≠а•Ба§Ѓа•На•§
а§Єа•На§§а•Ба§§а•На§µа§Њ а§Ъ а§ђа§єа•Ба§≠а§ња§Г а§Єа•На§§а•Ла§§а•На§∞а•Иа§Г а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§∞а•На§•а§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•Ба§∞а§Юа•На§Ьа§Єа§Ња••а•©а•™а••
а§¶а•За§µа§¶а•За§µ а§Ьа§Ча§®а•На§®а§Ња§• а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§Ња§Ша•Ма§Ша§µа§ња§®а§Ња§ґа§®а•§
а§Ьа§®а§Њ а§Еа§Єа•На§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•Б а§™а§Ња§™а§ња§Ја•На§†а§Ња§Г а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§§а•На§µа§Њ а§™а§Ња§™а§Ња§®а§њ а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§ґа§Га••а•©а•Ђа••
а§µа§ња§Єа•Га§Ьа•На§ѓ а§§а•На§µа§§а•На§™а§¶а§В а§ѓа§Ња§®а•На§§а§њ а§§а•На§µа§¶а§Ња§Ьа•На§Юа§Ња§Іа§Ња§∞а§ња§£а•Л а§≠а•Ба§µа§ња•§
а§Еа§Єа•На§Ѓа§Ња§Ха§В а§Ъа•Иа§µ а§§а§§а•На§™а§Ња§™а§В а§Ха§•а§В а§Ча§Ъа•На§Ыа•За§Ьа•На§Ьа§®а§Ња§∞а•На§¶а§®а••а•©а•ђа••
а§§а§¶а•Ба§™а§Ња§ѓа§В а§µа§¶а§Ња§Єа•На§Ѓа§Ња§Ха§В а§§а•На§µа§§а•На§™а§Ња§¶а§ґа§∞а§£а•Иа§Ја§ња§£а§Ња§Ѓа•На•§
а§За§§а§њ а§§а•Аа§∞а•На§•а•Иа§Г а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§∞а•На§•а§ња§§а§Єа•На§§а•Б а§≠а§Ча§µа§Ња§®а•На§≠а•Ва§§а§≠а§Ња§µа§®а§Га••а•©а•≠а••
а§™а•На§∞а§єа§Єа§®а•На§™а•На§∞а§Ња§є а§§а•Аа§∞а•На§•а§Ња§®а§њ а§Ѓа•За§Ша§Ча§Ѓа•На§≠а•Аа§∞а§ѓа§Њ а§Ча§ња§∞а§Ња•§
а§ґа•На§∞а•Аа§≠а§Ча§µа§Ња§®а•Ба§µа§Ња§Ъ—
а§Єа§ња§§а•З а§™а§Ха•На§Ја•З а§Ѓа•За§Ја§Єа•Ва§∞а•На§ѓа•З а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца§Ња§®а•На§§а•За§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За••а•©а•Ѓа••
а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§§а•Аа§∞а•На§•а§Ѓа§ѓа•З а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа•З а§Ѓа§Ѓа§Ња§ља§™а§њ а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§£а§µа§≤а•На§≤а§≠а•За•§
а§ѓа•Ва§ѓа§В а§≠а§Ча•Ла§¶а§ѓа§Ња§§а•На§™а•Ва§∞а•На§µа§В а§ђа§єа§ња§Га§Єа§Ва§Єа•На§•а§Ьа§≤а§Ња§™а•На§≤а•Ба§§а§Ња§Га••а•©а•ѓа••
а§µа§ња§Ѓа•Ба§Ха•На§§а§Ња§Ша§Ња§Г а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§∞а•Ва§™а§Њ а§≠а§µа§®а•На§§а•На§µа§Ња§ґа•Б а§Єа•Ба§®а§ња§∞а•На§Ѓа§≤а§Ња§Га•§
а§≠а§µа§¶а•На§≠а§ња§ґа•На§Ъ а§µа§ња§Ѓа•Ба§Ха•На§§а§Ња§Ша•Иа§∞а•На§ѓа•З а§® а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§§а§Њ а§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За••а•™а•¶а••
а§§а•За§Ја•Б а§§а§ња§Ја•На§†а§®а•На§§а•Б а§§а§§а•На§™а§Ња§™а§В а§Ьа§®а•Иа§∞а•На§ѓа•Ба§Ја•На§Ѓа§¶а•На§µа§ња§∞а•За§Ъа§ња§§а§Ѓа•На•§
а§За§§а§њ а§§а•Аа§∞а•На§•а§™а§¶а•Л а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§Єа•На§§а•Аа§∞а•На§•а§Ња§®а§Ња§В а§Ъ а§µа§∞а§В а§¶а§¶а•Ма••а•™а•Іа••
а§Еа§®а•Ба§Ьа•На§Юа§Ња§™а•На§ѓ а§Ъ а§§а§Ња§®а•На§ѓа•Ла§Ча§Ња§§а•На§§а§§а•На§∞а•Иа§µа§Ња§®а•На§§а§∞а§Іа•Аа§ѓа§§а•§
а§Єа•На§µа§Іа§Ња§Ѓа§Ња§®а§њ а§™а•Ба§®а§Г а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§™а•На§ѓ а§§а§Ња§®а§њ а§§а•Аа§∞а•На§•а§Ња§®а§њ а§®а§ња§§а•На§ѓа§ґа§Га••а•™а•®а••
а§™а•На§∞а§§а§ња§µа§∞а•На§Ја§В а§§а•Б а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца•З а§§а§•а•Иа§µа§Ња§®а•На§§а•На§ѓа§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§§а•За§®а§Ња§Ша•Ма§Ша§В а§µа§ња§Ѓа•Ба§Ъа•На§ѓа•Иа§µ а§ѓа§Ња§®а•На§§а§њ а§®а§ња§∞а•На§Ѓа§≤а§§а§Ња§Ѓа§єа•Ла••а•™а•©а••

а§Ча•Га§єа§Єа•На§•а§µа•На§∞а§§а§Ѓа•Н

а§™а§Ха•На§Ја§Ња§®а•На§§а§Њ а§Йа§™а§µа§Єа•На§§а§µа•На§ѓа§Ња§Г, а§™а§Ха•На§Ја§Ња§¶а§ѓа•Л а§ља§≠а§ња§ѓа§Ја•На§Яа§µа•На§ѓа§Ња§Га•§ а§Ха§ња§Юа•На§Ъ - ‘а§Еа§•а§Ња§™а§∞а§Ња§єа•На§£ а§Па§µа§Ња§™а•На§≤а•Ба§§а•На§ѓа•Ма§™а§µа§Єа§•а§ња§Ха§В а§¶а§Ѓа•На§™а§§а•А а§≠а•Ба§Юа•На§Ьа•Аа§ѓа§Ња§§а§Ња§В а§ѓа§¶а•Н а§Па§®а§ѓа•Ла§Г а§Ха§Ња§Ѓа•На§ѓа§В а§Єа•На§ѓа§Ња§§а•Н- а§Єа§∞а•На§™а§ња§∞а•Н а§Ѓа§ња§ґа•На§∞а§В а§Єа•На§ѓа§Ња§§а•Н а§Ха•Ба§ґа§≤а•За§®’а•§

‘а§Еа§ђа§єа•Ба§µа§Ња§¶а•А а§Єа•На§ѓа§Ња§§а•На•§ а§Єа§§а•На§ѓа§В а§µа§ња§µа§¶а§ња§Ја•За§§а•На•§ а§Еа§І а§Па§µа•Иа§§а§Ња§В а§∞а§Ња§§а•На§∞а§ња§В а§ґа§ѓа•Аа§ѓа§Ња§§а§Ња§Ѓа•На•§ а§§а•М а§Ца§≤а•Б а§Ьа§Ња§Ча•На§∞а§®а•Н-а§Ѓа§ња§ґа•На§∞а§Ња§µа•Н а§Па§µа•Иа§§а§Ња§В а§∞а§Ња§§а•На§∞а§ња§В а§µа§ња§єа§∞а•За§ѓа§Ња§§а§Ња§Ѓа•Н а§За§§а§ња§єа§Ња§Є-а§Ѓа§ња§ґа•На§∞а•За§£ а§µа§Њ а§Ха•За§®а§Ъа§ња§¶а•Н а§µа§Ња•§ а§Ьа•Ба§Ча•Ба§™а•На§Єа•За§ѓа§Ња§§а§Ња§В а§§а•На§µа•Н а§Па§µа§Ња§µа•На§∞а§§а•На§ѓа•За§≠а•На§ѓа§Г а§Ха§∞а•На§Ѓа§≠а•На§ѓа§Га•§’

а§™а§∞а•За§¶а•На§ѓа•Ба§∞а•Н а§єа•Ла§Ѓа§Ња§ѓ а§Єа§Ѓа•На§≠а§Ња§∞а§Єа§Ѓа•На§≠а§∞а§£а§Ња§¶а§ѓа•Л а§µа•На§ѓа§µа§Єа•На§•а§Ња§Г а§Ха§Ња§∞а•На§ѓа§Ња§Га•§

а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§ѓа§ґа•На§Ъа§ња§§а•На§§а§Ња§∞а•На§•а§Ѓа•Н а§Еа§™а§њ а§Ха§ња§Юа•На§Ъа§¶а•Н а§µа•На§∞а§§а§Ѓа•Н

“а§™а§∞а•На§µа§£а§њ а§µа§Њ а§§а§ња§≤а§≠а§Ха•На§Ј а§Йа§™а•Ла§Ја•На§ѓ а§µа§Њ а§ґа•На§µа•Ла§≠а•Ва§§ а§Йа§¶а§Ха§Ѓа•Ба§™а§Єа•На§™а•Га§ґа•На§ѓ а§Єа§Ња§µа§ња§§а•На§∞а•Аа§В а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§ґа§Г а§Єа§єа§Єа•На§∞а§Ха•Га§§а•На§µ а§Жа§µа§∞а•На§§а§ѓа•За§¶а•Н а§Еа§™а•На§∞а§Ња§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§ґа•Л а§µа§Њ” а§За§§а•На§ѓа•Н а§Жа§™а§Єа•На§§а§Ѓа•На§ђа§Іа§∞а•На§Ѓа§Єа•Ва§§а•На§∞а•За§Ја•Ба•§

а§™а§Юа•На§Ъ-а§™а§∞а•На§µ-а§™а•Ва§Ьа§Њ (а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§ња§Ѓа§Њ)

Observed on PaurбєЗamƒБsƒЂ tithi of every (lunar) month (ƒА≈ЫvinaбЄ•/paraviddha).

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: Pancha Parva
а§Єа§Ща•На§Ха•На§∞а§Ѓа§£-а§¶а§ња§®-а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§µа§Ња§єа•На§£-а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§Ха§Ња§≤а§Г
  • 05:57вЖТ12:16

When the transit of the Sun (saбєЕkrƒБnti) happens in the first half of the day (p≈ЂrvƒБhбєЗa) or after midnight of the previous day, the first half of the day is a puбєЗyakƒБla for various observances. In addition to 16 ghatikas on either side of the transition, and specific puбєЗyakƒБlas like бє£aбЄНa≈ЫƒЂti, viбє£бєЗupadƒЂ etc., this is also useful, especially in cases where the other puбєЗyakƒБlas are inconvenient for appropriate performance of rituals such as snƒБna, dƒБna, tarpaбєЗa, etc.

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: SunSankranti CommonFestivals
а§µа•За§Ща•На§Ха§Яа§Ња§Ъа§≤а•З а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§ња§Ѓа§Њ-а§Ча§∞а•Ба§°-а§Єа•За§µа§Њ

Observed on PaurбєЗamƒБsƒЂ tithi of every (lunar) month (Chandr≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

Lord Malayappa rides on Garuda Vahanam in all four mada streets. This usually happens during Brahmotsavams, but since 2006, this has been happening on every p≈ЂrбєЗimƒБ.

  • Tags: AndhraTempleFestivals

  • 05:57вЖТ11:36

VбєЫбє£abha-Ravi-SaбєЕkramaбєЗa-Viбє£бєЗupadƒЂ Punyakala.

Observed on Krћ•бє£бєЗa-PrathamƒБ tithi of every (lunar) month (P≈ЂrvƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/puurvaviddha). sthƒБlƒЂpakaбЄ• is an important fortnightly ritual, involving the offering of haviбє£yƒБnna to agni, with the offering being cooked on the fire itself.

а§µа§Ња§Єа§Ња§ѓа§њ-а§ѓа•Ба§¶а•На§І-а§Єа§Ѓа§Ња§™а•На§§а§ња§Г #а•®а•Ѓа•©

Event occured on 1739-05-16 (gregorian). Julian date was converted to Gregorian in this reckoning.

Chimaji Appa ground down the Portuguese, systematically mined, blasted and won vasAi/ bessein (long suffering Christian inquisition barbarisms).

Impact

This ended the Portuguese “Generalship of the North”. The resulting treaty ensured that Gentoos (Hindus) inhabiting Goa etc. could again freely practice their religion. The English (having failed to aid Portuguese) hastily sent envoys to draw up friendship treaties with marATha-s. This victory came at a heavy cost (22k soldiers dead) and after a determined campaign of 2 years.

Events

“On the 3rd, the tower of San Sebastian was demolished by a Maratha mine. Eight hundred Portuguese soldiers had been killed and their powerful bastions of San Sebastian and Remedios lay demolished. The white flag was hoisted on the fort and on the 5th of May 1739, the garrison surrendered. Ten days later, they were allowed to march out of Vasai into boats which would take them to Goa. On the 23rd of May 1739, the Portuguese flag which had flown atop Vasai or Bacaim as the Portuguese called it, was pulled down and the zari parka unfurled in it’s place. A bell from the church on the fort was taken and re installed as a temple bell at Bhimashankar! Other bells were sent to other temples.” - AG’s book.

Details

  • Tags:
а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ц-а§Ѓа§Ња§Є-а§Еа§®а•На§§а§ња§Ѓа§§а•На§∞а§ѓа§§а§ња§•а§њ-а§µа•На§∞а§§-а§Єа§Ѓа§Ња§™а§®а§Ѓа•Н

Observed on PaurбєЗamƒБsƒЂ tithi of Vai≈ЫƒБkhaбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

Last three days of vai≈ЫƒБkha-mƒБsaбЄ•. anantaphaladam! Perform Shiva/Vishnu Puja, Bhagavata Parayanam, BhagavadGita Parayanam, Vishnu Sahasranama Parayanam, dƒБna of ap≈Ђpa, pƒБyasa etc.

а§¶а•На§µа§Ња§¶а§ґа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§™а§Ња§≤а§ња§§а§В а§§а§Ъа•На§Ъ а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§®а§Њ а§™а•На§∞а§≠а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§®а§Ња•§
а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•Ла§¶а§ґа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§Єа•Ба§Іа§Ња§В а§¶а•За§µа§Ња§®а•На§™а§Ња§ѓа§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§Ња§Є а§µа•И а§єа§∞а§ња§Га••а•ђа••
а§Ьа§Ша§Ња§® а§Ъ а§Ъа§§а•Ба§∞а•На§¶а§ґа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§¶а•Иа§§а•На§ѓа§Ња§®а•На§¶а•За§µа§µа§ња§∞а•Ла§Іа§ња§®а§Га•§
а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§В а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§¶а•За§µа§Ња§®а§Ња§В а§Єа§Ња§Ѓа•На§∞а§Ња§Ьа•На§ѓа§Ња§ља§ља§™а•На§§а§ња§∞а•На§ђа§≠а•Ва§µ а§єа••а•≠а••
а§§а§§а•Л а§¶а•За§µа§Ња§Г а§Єа•Ба§Єа§®а•На§§а•Ба§Ја•На§Яа§Њ а§Па§§а§Ња§Єа§Ња§В а§Ъ а§µа§∞а§В а§¶а§¶а•Ба§Га•§
а§§а§ња§Єа•Га§£а§Ња§В а§Ъ а§§а§ња§•а•Аа§®а§Ња§В а§µа•И а§™а•На§∞а•Аа§§а•На§ѓа•Ла§§а•На§Ђа•Ба§≤а•На§≤а§µа§ња§≤а•Ла§Ъа§®а§Ња§Га••а•Ѓа••
а§Па§§а§Њ а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§Єа•На§ѓ а§§а§ња§Єа•На§∞а§ґа•На§Ъ а§§а§ња§•а§ѓа§Г а§ґа•Ба§≠а§Ња§Га•§
а§™а•Ба§§а•На§∞а§™а•Ма§§а•На§∞а§Ња§¶а§ња§Ђа§≤а§¶а§Њ а§®а§∞а§Ња§£а§Ња§В а§™а§Ња§™а§єа§Ња§®а§ња§¶а§Ња§Га••а•ѓа••
а§ѓа•Ла§ља§Єа•На§Ѓа§ња§®а•На§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•З а§Ъ а§Єа§Ѓа•На§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а•З а§® а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§§а•Л а§Ѓа§®а•Ба§Ьа§Ња§Іа§Ѓа§Га•§
а§§а§ња§•а§ња§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•З а§§а•Б а§Є а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§§а•На§µа§Њ а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§Ѓа•За§µ а§Ђа§≤а§В а§≤а§≠а•За§§а•На••а•Іа•¶а••
а§§а§ња§•а§ња§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За§™а•На§ѓа§Ха•Ба§∞а•На§µа§Ња§£а§Г а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§®а§¶а§Ња§®а§Ња§¶а§ња§Ха§В а§®а§∞а§Га•§
а§Ъа§Ња§£а•На§°а§Ња§≤а•Аа§В а§ѓа•Ла§®а§ња§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§Ња§¶а•На§ѓ а§™а§ґа•На§Ъа§Ња§¶а•На§∞а•Ма§∞а§µа§Ѓа§ґа•На§®а•Ба§§а•За••а•Іа•Іа••
а§Йа§Ја•На§£а•Ла§¶а§Ха•За§® а§ѓа§Г а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§§а§њ а§Ѓа§Ња§Іа§µа•З а§Ъ а§§а§ња§•а§ња§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§∞а•Ма§∞а§µа§В а§®а§∞а§Ха§В а§ѓа§Ња§§а§њ а§ѓа§Ња§µа§¶а§ња§Ва§¶а•На§∞а§Ња§ґа•На§Ъа§§а•Ба§∞а•На§¶а§ґа••а•Іа•®а••
а§™а§ња§§а•Да§®а•На§¶а•За§µа§Ња§®а•На§Єа§Ѓа•Ба§¶а•На§¶а§ња§ґа•На§ѓ а§¶а§Іа•На§ѓа§®а•На§®а§В а§® а§¶а§¶а§Ња§§а§њ а§ѓа§Га•§
а§™а•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ъа•Аа§В а§ѓа•Ла§®а§ња§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§Ња§¶а•На§ѓ а§§а§ња§Ја•На§†а§§а•На§ѓа§Ња§≠а•Ва§§а§Єа§Ѓа•На§™а•На§≤а§µа§Ѓа•На••а•Іа•©а••
а§™а•На§∞а§µа•Га§§а•На§§а§Ња§®а§Ња§В а§Ъ а§Ха§Ња§Ѓа§Ња§®а§Ња§В а§Ѓа§Ња§Іа§µа•З а§®а§ња§ѓа§Ѓа•З а§Ха•Га§§а•За•§
а§Еа§µа§ґа•На§ѓа§В а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§Єа§Ња§ѓа•Ба§Ьа•На§ѓа§В а§ѓа•Ба§Ьа•На§ѓа§§а•З а§®а§Ња§§а•На§∞ а§Єа§Ва§ґа§ѓа§Га••а•Іа•™а••
а§Жа§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§В а§®а§ња§ѓа§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§Ха•На§§а§Г а§Ха•Ба§∞а•На§ѓа§Ња§¶а•На§ѓа§¶а§њ а§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§§а•За§® а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§Ђа§≤а§В а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§™а•На§ѓ а§Ѓа•Ла§¶а§§а•З а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§Ѓа§®а•На§¶а§ња§∞а•За••а•Іа•Ђа••
а§ѓа•Л а§µа•И а§¶а•За§µа§Ња§®а•На§™а§ња§§а•Да§®а•На§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§В а§Ча•Ба§∞а•Ба§Ѓа•Ба§¶а•На§¶а§ња§ґа•На§ѓ а§Ѓа§Ња§®а§µа§Га•§
а§® а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§®а§Ња§¶а§њ а§Ха§∞а•Ла§§а•На§ѓа§¶а•На§Іа§Ња§ља§Ѓа•Ба§Ја•На§ѓ а§ґа§Ња§™а§™а•На§∞а§¶а§Њ а§µа§ѓа§Ѓа•На••а•Іа•ђа••
а§®а§ња§Га§Єа§®а•На§§а§Ња§®а•Л а§®а§ња§∞а§Ња§ѓа•Ба§ґа•На§Ъ а§®а§ња§Га§ґа•На§∞а•За§ѓа§Єа•На§Ха•Л а§≠а§µа•За§¶а§ња§§а§ња•§
а§За§§а§њ а§¶а•За§µа§Њ а§µа§∞а§В а§¶а§§а•На§§а•На§µа§Њ а§Єа•На§µа§Іа§Ња§Ѓа§Ња§®а§њ а§ѓа§ѓа•Ба§Г а§™а•Ба§∞а§Ња••а•Іа•≠а••
а§§а§Єа•На§Ѓа§Ња§§а•На§§а§ња§•а§ња§§а•На§∞а§ѓа§В а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§В а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§Ња§Ша•Ма§Ша§µа§ња§®а§Ња§ґа§®а§Ѓа•На•§
а§Еа§®а•На§§а•На§ѓа§В а§™а•Ба§Ја•На§Ха§∞а§ња§£а•Аа§Єа§Ва§Ьа•На§Юа§В а§™а•Ба§§а•На§∞а§™а•Ма§§а•На§∞а§µа§ња§µа§∞а•На§Іа§®а§Ѓа•На••а•Іа•Ѓа••
а§ѓа§Њ а§®а§Ња§∞а•А а§Єа•Ба§≠а§Ча§Ња§ља§ља§™а•Ва§™а§™а§Ња§ѓа§Єа§В а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§ња§Ѓа§Ња§¶а§ња§®а•За•§
а§ђа•На§∞а§Ња§єа•На§Ѓа§£а§Ња§ѓ а§Єа§Ха•Га§¶а•На§¶а§¶а•На§ѓа§Ња§§а•На§Ха•Аа§∞а•На§§а§ња§Ѓа§®а•На§§а§В а§Єа•Ба§§а§В а§≤а§≠а•За§§а•На••а•Іа•ѓа••
а§Ча•Аа§§а§Ња§™а§Ња§†а§В а§§а•Б а§ѓа§Г а§Ха•Ба§∞а•На§ѓа§Ња§¶а§®а•На§§а§ња§Ѓа•З а§Ъ а§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§¶а§ња§®а•За§¶а§ња§®а•За§ља§ґа•На§µа§Ѓа•За§Іа§Ња§®а§Ња§В а§Ђа§≤а§Ѓа•За§§а§њ а§® а§Єа§Ва§ґа§ѓа§Га••а•®а•¶а••
а§Єа§єа§Єа•На§∞а§®а§Ња§Ѓа§™а§†а§®а§В а§ѓа§Г а§Ха•Ба§∞а•На§ѓа•На§ѓа§Ња§Ъа•На§Ъ а§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§§а§Єа•На§ѓ а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§Ђа§≤а§В а§µа§Ха•На§§а•Ба§В а§Ха§Г а§ґа§Ха•На§§а•Л а§¶а§ња§µа§њ а§µа§Њ а§≠а•Ба§µа§ња••а•®а•Іа••
а§Єа§єа§Єа•На§∞а§®а§Ња§Ѓа§≠а§ња§∞а•На§¶а•За§µа§В а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§В а§Ѓа§Іа•Ба§Єа•Ва§¶а§®а§Ѓа•На•§
а§™а§ѓа§Єа§Њ а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§™а•На§ѓ а§µа•И а§ѓа§Ња§§а§њ а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§≤а•Ла§Ха§Ѓа§Ха§≤а•На§Ѓа§Ја§Ѓа•На••а•®а•®а••
а§Єа§Ѓа§Єа•На§§а§µа§ња§≠а§µа•Иа§∞а•На§ѓа§Єа•На§§а•Б а§™а•Ва§Ьа§ѓа•За§®а•На§Ѓа§Іа•Ба§Єа•Ва§¶а§®а§Ѓа•На•§
а§® а§§а§Єа•На§ѓ а§≤а•Ла§Ха§Ња§Г а§Ха•На§Ја•Аа§ѓа§®а•На§§а•З а§ѓа•Ба§Ч а§Ха§≤а•На§™а§Ња§¶а§њ а§µа•На§ѓа§§а•На§ѓа§ѓа•За••а•®а•©а••
а§Еа§Єа•На§®а§Ња§§а•На§µа§Њ а§Ъа§Ња§™а•На§ѓа§¶а§§а•На§§а•На§µа§Њ а§Ъ а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца§ґа•На§Ъ а§Ѓа§§а•Л а§ѓа§¶а§ња•§
а§Є а§ђа•На§∞а§єа•На§Ѓа§єа§Њ а§Ча•Ба§∞а•Ба§Ша•На§®а§ґа•На§Ъ а§™а§ња§§а•Да§£а§Ња§В а§Ша§Ња§§а§Ха§Єа•На§§а§•а§Ња••а•®а•™а••
а§ґа•На§≤а•Ла§Ха§Ња§∞а•На§Іа§В а§ґа•На§≤а•Ла§Ха§™а§Ња§¶а§В а§µа§Њ а§®а§ња§§а•На§ѓа§В а§≠а§Ња§Ча§µа§§а•Ла§¶а•На§≠а§µа§Ѓа•На•§
а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца•З а§Ъ а§™а§†а§®а•На§Ѓа§∞а•На§§а•На§ѓа•Л а§ђа•На§∞а§єа•На§Ѓа§§а•На§µа§В а§Ъа•Ла§™а§™а§¶а•На§ѓа§§а•За••а•®а•Ђа••
а§ѓа•Л а§µа•И а§≠а§Ња§Ча§µа§§а§В а§ґа§Ња§Єа•На§§а•На§∞а§В а§ґа•Га§£а•Ла§§а•На§ѓа•За§§а§¶а•На§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§® а§™а§Ња§™а•Иа§∞а•На§≤а§ња§™а•На§ѓа§§а•З а§Ха•На§µа§Ња§ља§™а§њ а§™а§¶а•На§Ѓа§™а§§а•На§∞а§Ѓа§ња§µа§Ња§Ва§≠а§Єа§Ња••а•®а•ђа••
а§¶а•За§µа§§а•На§µа§В а§Ѓа§®а•Ба§Ьа•Иа§Г а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§™а•На§§а§В а§Ха•Иа§ґа•На§Ъа§ња§§а•На§Єа§ња§¶а•На§Іа§§а•На§µа§Ѓа•За§µ а§Ъа•§
а§Ха•Иа§ґа•На§Ъа§ња§§а•На§™а•На§∞а§Ња§™а•На§§а•Л а§ђа•На§∞а§єа•На§Ѓа§≠а§Ња§µа•Л а§¶а§ња§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа§®а§ња§Ја•За§µа§£а§Ња§§а•На••а•®а•≠а••
а§ђа•На§∞а§єа•На§Ѓа§Ьа•На§Юа§Ња§®а•За§® а§µа•И а§Ѓа•Ба§Ха•На§§а§ња§Г а§™а•На§∞а§ѓа§Ња§Ча§Ѓа§∞а§£а•За§® а§µа§Ња•§
а§Еа§•а§µа§Њ а§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§њ а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца•З а§®а§ња§ѓа§Ѓа•За§® а§Ьа§≤а§Ња§™а•На§≤а•Ба§§а•За§Га••а•®а•Ѓа••
а§®а•Аа§≤а§µа•Га§Ја§В а§Єа§Ѓа•Ба§§а•На§Єа•Га§Ьа•На§ѓ а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§Ъ а§Ьа§≤а§Ња§™а•На§≤а•Ба§§а•За§Га•§
а§Єа§Ѓа§Єа•На§§а§ђа§®а•На§Іа§®а§ња§∞а•На§Ѓа•Ба§Ха•На§§а§Г а§™а•Ба§Ѓа§Ња§®а•На§ѓа§Ња§§а§њ а§™а§∞а§В а§™а§¶а§Ѓа•На••а•®а•ѓа••
а§Ча§Ња§В а§Єа§µа§§а•На§Єа§Ња§В а§¶а•На§µа§ња§Ьа•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞а§Ња§ѓ а§Єа•Аа§¶а§§а•З а§Ъ а§Ха•Ба§Яа•Ба§Ѓа•На§ђа§ња§®а•За•§
а§За§єа§Ња§™а§Ѓа•Га§§а•На§ѓа•Ба§®а§ња§∞а•На§Ѓа•Ба§Ха•На§§а§Г а§™а§∞а§§а•На§∞ а§Ъ а§™а§∞а§В а§µа•На§∞а§Ьа•За§§а•На••а•©а•¶а••
а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§®а§¶а§Ња§®а§µа§ња§єа•Аа§®а§Єа•На§§а•Б а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ца•А а§Ъа•Иа§µ а§ѓа•Л а§®а§ѓа•За§§а•На•§
а§ґа•На§µа§Ња§®а§ѓа•Ла§®а§ња§ґа§§а§В а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§™а•На§ѓ а§µа§ња§Ја•На§†а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§В а§Ьа§Ња§ѓа§§а•З а§Ха•Га§Ѓа§ња§Га••а•©а•Іа••
а§§а§ња§Єа•На§∞а§Г а§Ха•Ла§Яа•На§ѓа•Ла§ља§∞а•На§Іа§Ха•Ла§Яа§ња§ґа•На§Ъ а§§а•Аа§∞а•На§•а§Ња§®а§њ а§≠а•Ба§µа§®а§§а•На§∞а§ѓа•За•§
а§Єа§Ѓа•На§≠а•Ва§ѓ а§Ѓа§®а•На§§а•На§∞а§ѓа§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа§Ха•На§∞а•Ба§Г а§™а§Ња§™а§Єа§Ща•На§Ша§Ња§§а§ґа§Ща•На§Ха§ња§§а§Ња§Га••а•©а•®а••
а§Ьа§®а§Њ а§Еа§Єа•На§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•Б а§™а§Ња§™а§ња§Ја•На§†а§Њ а§µа§ња§Єа•Га§Ьа§®а•На§§а§њ а§Єа•На§µа§Ха§В а§Ѓа§≤а§Ѓа•На•§
а§§а§¶а§Єа•На§Ѓа§Ња§Ха§В а§Ха§•а§В а§Ча§Ъа•На§Ыа•За§¶а§ња§§а§њ а§Ъа§ња§®а•На§§а§Ња§Єа§Ѓа§®а•На§µа§ња§§а§Ња§Га••а•©а•©а••
а§§а•Аа§∞а•На§•а§™а§Ња§¶а§В а§єа§∞а§ња§В а§Ьа§Ча•На§Ѓа•Ба§Г а§ґа§∞а§£а•На§ѓа§В а§ґа§∞а§£а§В а§µа§ња§≠а•Ба§Ѓа•На•§
а§Єа•На§§а•Ба§§а•На§µа§Њ а§Ъ а§ђа§єа•Ба§≠а§ња§Г а§Єа•На§§а•Ла§§а•На§∞а•Иа§Г а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§∞а•На§•а§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•Ба§∞а§Юа•На§Ьа§Єа§Ња••а•©а•™а••
а§¶а•За§µа§¶а•За§µ а§Ьа§Ча§®а•На§®а§Ња§• а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§Ња§Ша•Ма§Ша§µа§ња§®а§Ња§ґа§®а•§
а§Ьа§®а§Њ а§Еа§Єа•На§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•Б а§™а§Ња§™а§ња§Ја•На§†а§Ња§Г а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§§а•На§µа§Њ а§™а§Ња§™а§Ња§®а§њ а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§ґа§Га••а•©а•Ђа••

а§µа•Иа§ґа§Ња§Ц-а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§ња§Ѓа§Њ-а§Єа•На§®а§Ња§®а§Ѓа•Н

Observed on PaurбєЗamƒБsƒЂ tithi of Vai≈ЫƒБkhaбЄ• (lunar) month (PrƒБktanƒБruбєЗ≈НdayaбЄ•/paraviddha).

Perform snana four ghatikas before sunrise (during aruбєЗ≈НdayaбЄ•, when the Moon is still up, on the full-moon days of ƒБбє£ƒБбЄНha, kƒБrttika, mƒБgha and vai≈ЫƒБkha вАУ hence the name ƒБ-kƒБ-mƒБ-vai) bestows strength, beauty, fame, dharma, knowledge, happiness, fortitude and health.

Observed on M≈ЂlƒБ nakshatra of Vrћ•бє£abhaбЄ• (sidereal solar) month (PrƒБtaбЄ•/paraviddha). The event occurred in 5084 (Kali era).

jagadguru ≈ЫrƒЂ~≈ЫaбєЕkara vijayƒУndra sarasvatƒЂ was inducted into the kƒБmak≈Нбє≠i-pƒЂбє≠ham on 29th May 1983, rudhir≈НdgƒБrƒЂ year, vrћ•бє£abha/vai≈ЫƒБkha mƒБsa krћ•бє£бєЗa trћ•tƒЂyƒБ bhƒБnuvƒБsaraбЄ• m≈ЂlƒБ nakбє£atram.

а§™а§Ња§∞а•На§•а§ња§µ-а§Ха§≤а•На§™а§Ња§¶а§ња§Г

Observed on Krћ•бє£бєЗa-Trћ•tƒЂyƒБ tithi of Vai≈ЫƒБkhaбЄ• (lunar) month (AparƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/vyaapti).

pƒБrthiva-kalpƒБdiбЄ• is observed on this day. Performing ≈ЫrƒБddha on this day gives akбє£ayatrћ•pti to pitrs. Although there are 30 Kalpadis described in various puranas, 7 of them are particularly special.

а•®а•¶а•¶а•¶-а§§а§Ѓа•З а§µа§∞а•На§Ја•З а•Юа§ња§Ьа§њ-а§¶а•На§µа•Аа§™а•Ла§™а§™а•На§≤а§µа§Г #а•®а•®

Event occured on 2000-05-19 (gregorian).

On this day, Indigenous Christian Fijians led by failed businessman George Speight effected a coup, triggering the removal of the Hindu-fijian led multi-ethnic government.

Context
  • Since the 1987 coup, many Indo Fijians had fled. In 1997, racially discriminatory provisions in the constitution were removed. A Hindu-led multi-ethnic government formed.
  • Many Indigenous Christian Fijians did not liek this. They were additionally supported by the Church and the native Chiefs.
  • The Indians had continued to neglect participation in the Army for many decades (Their demanding equal pay as Euroeans during WW2 caused the British to disband the Indian platoon).
  • Also, Indian Total Fertility Rate (which was initially higher than the natives) had fallen.
Aftermath
  • The ensuing chaos caused the military under Commodore Frank Bainimarama to effect coup and restore order. They scrapped the 1997 constitution. This was reversed in the courts. However, succeeding govrnments (via Reconciliation Tolerance and Unity Bill, Qoliqoli Bill and the Land Tribunal Bill) were lenient towards the initial usurpers.
  • In 2006, after ultimatums which were ignored, Commodore Frank Bainimarama and aides effected a military coup, clipped the wings of the Methodist Church and Council of Chiefs, and eventually rewrote the constitution replacing the race-based electoral system with a proportional representation system.
Details

  • Tags:

а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•©а•¶ а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§ђа•Ла§Іа•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а•® а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•Іа•©а•ђа•Ѓ

Observed on Krћ•бє£бєЗa-CaturthƒЂ tithi of Vai≈ЫƒБkhaбЄ• (lunar) month (AparƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/vyaapti). The event occurred in 3756 (Kali era).

The preceptor BƒБlabodhendra was the son of KƒБlahasti, and was called BƒБlaya, bereft of ignorance, attained the Supreme state of immortality on the fourth day of the dark fortnight of the month of Vai≈ЫƒБka in the year ƒАnanda. His preceptorship was for thirty-seven years. He attained siddhi in KƒБ√±ci.

а§Ха§ґа•На§ѓа§™-а§Ѓа§єа§∞а•На§Ја§њ-а§Ьа§ѓа§®а•На§§а•А

Observed on Krћ•бє£бєЗa-Pa√±camƒЂ tithi of Vai≈ЫƒБkhaбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

а§Єа§Ња§ѓа§®-а§µа•Иа§Іа•Га§§а§ња§Г
  • вЖТ19:09

When the sum of the longitudes of the Moon and Sun is 360, it is sƒБyana-vaidhrћ•tiбЄ•, and a y≈НgaбЄ• that is to be excluded for auspicious activities.

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: Combinations
а§§а•Иа§Ѓа•Ва§∞а•За§£ а§¶а•За§єа§≤а•А-а§Ча•На§∞а§єа§£а§Ѓа•Н #а•ђа•®а•™

Event occured on 1398-05-20 (gregorian). Julian date was converted to Gregorian in this reckoning.

On this day, Taimur routed the tughlaq army. He then entered delhi and massacred hindus.

Events (via MT)

Timur triumphantly marched into Delhi and the Ulema begged him to spare the lives of the Moslems. He asked them to proclaim him the exalted sultan of Hindustan. The Hindus seeing that they faced a brutal death revolted enmasse and were slaughtered with much fury in the fierce fighting that broke out through the streets of Delhi. Four pyramids of the heads of slaughtered Hindus were set up in the four corner of Delhi and only the qualified craftsmen were bound and sent off in slave trains to Samarqand.

Any Moslems who failed to give TimurвАЩs troops their supplies were also forthwith roasted like Kebobs.

Timur spent 15 days in Delhi solemnly occupying the throne of Delhi declaring himself emperor of India. He summoned 120 elephants and made them bow their heads and kneel before him in obeisance and trumpet in unison. He felt that it marked the submission of Hindustan itself at the feet of the world conqueror. He then sent off the elephants in long strings to the Herat, Tabriz, Shiraz and Samarqand. The treasury was taken by Timur and in one stroke the wealth that the Moslem rulers had robbed from Indians over two centuries, comprising of incalculable amounts of gold, silver and gems.

He then performed his Islamic prayers in the old Jami Masjid, placed a cleric from Bokhara as its Imam and had him read the Friday Namaz in his name. Finally on January 1, 1399 when the stench of the corpses made his stay impossible, he ordered his troops to burn down Delhi, except for the Moslem quarters, and proceeded to attack Meerut.

Context

Timur was on his way to India from the Tughlaqs, who had weakened.

Pakistani army with local Islamists massacre Hindus in Bengal at Sendia, Galimpur, Chuknagar

а§∞а§µа§њ-а§Єа§Ща•На§Ха•На§∞а§Ѓа§£-а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§Ха§Ња§≤а§Г
  • 05:56вЖТ13:16

16 ghatikas on either side of the transition of the Sun are sacred for various observances. In addition to specific puбєЗyakƒБlas like бє£aбЄНa≈ЫƒЂti, viбє£бєЗupadƒЂ etc., this is also useful, especially in cases where the other puбєЗyakƒБlas are inconvenient for appropriate performance of rituals such as snƒБna, dƒБna, tarpaбєЗa, etc.

а§Єа§Ња§ѓа§®-а§Єа§Ща•На§Ха•На§∞а§Ѓа§£-а§¶а§ња§®-а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§µа§Ња§єа•На§£-а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§Ха§Ња§≤а§Г
  • 05:56вЖТ12:16

When the transit of the Sun (saбєЕkrƒБnti) happens in the first half of the day (p≈ЂrvƒБhбєЗa) or after midnight of the previous day, the first half of the day is a puбєЗyakƒБla for various observances. In addition to 16 ghatikas on either side of the transition, and specific puбєЗyakƒБlas like бє£aбЄНa≈ЫƒЂti, viбє£бєЗupadƒЂ etc., this is also useful, especially in cases where the other puбєЗyakƒБlas are inconvenient for appropriate performance of rituals such as snƒБna, dƒБna, tarpaбєЗa, etc.

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: SunSankranti CommonFestivals
а§ґа•На§∞а§µа§£-а§µа•На§∞а§§а§Ѓа•Н

Observed on every occurrence of ≈ЪravaбєЗaбЄ• nakshatra (SƒБбєЕgavaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: SpecialVratam
а§ґа•Ба§Ха•На§∞-а§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§Г/а§Ча•На§∞а•Аа§Ја•На§Ѓа§Ла§§а•Ба§Г
  • 06:52вЖТ

Beginning of ≈Ыukra-mƒБsa, marked by the transit of Sun into mithuna-rƒБshƒЂ. Also marks the beginning of grƒЂбє£marћ•tuбЄ•. While nirayana saбєЕkrƒБnti’s are widely observed, tropical saбєЕkrƒБnti’s are also equally sacred, and have similar puбєЗyakƒБlas associated with them.

а§≠а§Ња§®а•Ба§Єа§™а•На§§а§Ѓа•А

saptamƒЂ tithi on a Sunday is as sacred as a solar eclipse. Particularly good for worshipping Surya.

Observed on day 8 of Vrћ•бє£abhaбЄ• (sidereal solar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

Enactment of restoration of eyesight of Shukracharya.

а§™а§Юа•На§Ъ-а§™а§∞а•На§µ-а§™а•Ва§Ьа§Њ (а§Еа§Ја•На§Яа§Ѓа•А)

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: Pancha Parva
а§ґа§ња§µа§∞а§Ња§Ьа•Ма§∞а§Ща•На§Ча•Ыа•За§ђ-а§Ѓа•За§≤а§®а§Ѓа•Н #а•©а•Ђа•ђ

Event occured on 1666-05-22 (gregorian). Julian date was converted to Gregorian in this reckoning.

shivAjI and sambhAjI were brought to the emperor by rAmasimha son of jayasiMha. They sent their nazar and nisAr gifts to awrangzeb. Everyone was given pAn from Awrang’s receng birthday. Awrangzeb did not say anything to them. By this time, shivAjI was very angry and awrangzeb told rAm singh to find out what the matter was. shivAjI said: “You have seen, your father has seen, your Emperor has seen what a man I am, and yet you have deliberately kept me standing! I cast off your mansab. If you wanted me to stand, you should have done it with proper thought.” Then he turned his back to the Emperor and immediately began to walk away. Whereupon Ram Sigh caught him by the hand, but Shivaji wrenched it away, came to one side and sat down. Ram Singh followed and tried to conciliate him. But Shivaji refused to listen and exclaimed: “… Cut off my head and take it there if you like; but I am not going into the Emperor’s presence again.” … Awrang sent multafat, aqil and mukhlis khAns with a robe to conciliate him; but he still refused. Finally, Awrang told rAmasimha to take him to his residence and conciliate him. (As related by by jayasiMha’s agent parkAldAs to kalyAndAs in a letter.)

It was well established Mogol custom that no one except the emperor and perhaps a crown prince were given a seat; and shivAjI knew it. But being made to stand below lackeys like jasvant singh. He was most angry with himself for agreeing to this abject visit in the first place. He had just agreed to the treaty and visit as expediency towards his goal of svarAjya, but this was too much. Awrangzeb saw him as a mere recalciterant rebel (mountain rat); while shivAjI knew he was far loftier than the average mogol nobleman.

In the coming days, shivAjI refused to go to Awrangzeb again, though he relented and sent his son to stand beside rAmasimha one day. rAmasiMha made excuses like “He has a fever”.

а§Йа§§а•На§Єа§µа§Ња§Г

  • а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•≠ а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§Жа§®а§®а•На§¶а§Ьа•На§Юа§Ња§®а•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•®а•¶а•≠а•≠, а§ђа§®а•На§¶а§Њ-а§ґа§ња§Ја•На§ѓа§Ња§Г а§Ыа§™а•На§™а§∞а§Ыа§ња§∞а•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§Ьа§ѓа§®а•На§§а§њ #а•©а•Іа•®, а§µа•Иа§Іа•Га§§а§њ-а§ґа•На§∞а§Ња§¶а•На§Іа§Ѓа•Н
а§ђа§®а•На§¶а§Њ-а§ґа§ња§Ја•На§ѓа§Ња§Г а§Ыа§™а•На§™а§∞а§Ыа§ња§∞а•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§Ьа§ѓа§®а•На§§а§њ #а•©а•Іа•®

Event occured on 1710-05-23 (gregorian). Julian date was converted to Gregorian in this reckoning.

Banda Bahadur, the vaiShNava disciple of Guru Gobind, crushed a mogol army. The Mughals were armed with artillery, well equipped cavalry and large infantry whereas the Sikhs had cavalry and infantry but no artillery. In the battle, his Sikhs gave a crushing blow to the Mughal empire. Wazir Khan (Sirhind) was killed in the battle and Sikhs established their first Raj in Punjab.

The bandai khAlsa was heavily hinduised (eg. vegetarianism, red dress).

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags:
а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•≠ а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§Жа§®а§®а•На§¶а§Ьа•На§Юа§Ња§®а•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•®а•¶а•≠а•≠

Observed on Krћ•бє£бєЗa-NavamƒЂ tithi of Vai≈ЫƒБkhaбЄ• (lunar) month (AparƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/vyaapti). The event occurred in 3047 (Kali era).

A native of Chera country, son of S≈ЂryanƒБrƒБyaбєЗamakhi, by name Chinnaya, having secured excellence in SƒБhitya by the infinite or immeasurable or unbound grace of goddess GaurƒЂ, who was pleased with worship, the sage by name ƒАnƒБndaj√±ƒБna prefixed with the term вАШBhagavatвАЩ the author of a collection of expository texts on the works of ƒАcƒБrya, lived in the KƒБmakoбє≠i PƒЂбє≠ha of the ƒАcƒБrya, the author of a commentary on Brahmas≈Ђtras. This accomplished knower of Brahman having got rid of the ignorance by the immaculate splendour of the moon-like ≈ЪuddhƒБnandamunƒЂndra, spreading the path of non-dualism protecting the world/earth for sixty-nine years, then approaching ≈ЪrƒЂ≈ЫailƒБ, on the way, attained blissful liberation on the ninth day of the dark fortnight in the month of RƒБdhƒБ (Vai≈ЫƒБkha) of the year Krodhana.

Event occured on 1737-05-24 (gregorian).

On Saturday at 11 p.m. the Mahrattas captured a water-tower in Thana creek and entered Sal√Іette. On April 6 and 7, the Mahratta troops practically occupied the whole island.

а§єа§®а•Ба§Ѓа§¶а•Н-а§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б-а§Ѓа§∞а§£а§Ѓа•Н #а•®а•©

Event occured on 1999-05-24 (gregorian).

On this day died the master wrestler Vijay Pal Yadav (1901вАУ1999) aka Guru Hanuman in a car crash. Guru Hanuman was fit and actively coaching, consuming large amounts of ghee and milk, even in his late 90s. He died on 24 May 1999, at the age of 98, not of old age or any ailments, but in a car crash! He was on his way to Haridwar to bathe in the holy Ganga.

He founded an akhada to train wrestlers in 1925 with the help of K. K. Birla. Guru Hanuman coached or mentored the brightest Indian wrestlers for decades. His students got Arjuna Awards, won medals at Asian Games and Commonwealth Games, and represented India at the Olympics. After K. D. JadhavвАЩs bronze in 1952, India did not have a wrestling medal in Olympics for 56 years. Then came Sushil Kumar, a вАШpra≈Ыiбє£yaвАЩ (grand-disciple) of Guru Hanuman. Sushil Kumar was coached by Satpal Singh, a disciple of Guru Hanuman. Sushil Kumar won a bronze in 2008 and silver in 2012. Yogeshwar Dutt (bronze in 2012) also trained under Satpal Singh. So did Ravi Kumar Dahiya (silver in 2020).

Sources: nityAnanda mishra’s tweets.

Event occured on 1657-05-24 (gregorian). Julian date was converted to Gregorian in this reckoning.

jyeShTha s12 Sambhaji born

а§ґа§ња§µа§∞а§Ња§Ьа•Л а§∞а§Ња§Ѓа§Єа§ња§Ва§єа•За§® а§∞а§Ха•На§Ја§ња§§а§Г #а•©а•Ђа•ђ

Event occured on 1666-05-24 (gregorian). Julian date was converted to Gregorian in this reckoning.

On this day, rAmasiMha, son fo jayasiMha, protected shivAjI’s life.

Those like begam jahAn-ara, javar khAn and jaswant singh kept egging awrangzeb after shivAjI had angrily left awrangzeb’s darbaar. Awrangzeb ordered siddi fulAd to take shivAjI to Rad-andaz Khan’s mansion. rAmasiMha, son of jayasiMha, heard of it. He at once rushed to Muhammad Amin Khan’s house and interceded with him saying: “The Emperor is thinking of killing Shivaji. But he has come here under a solemn assurance of safe conduct (kaul). So it is proper that the Emperor should first kill me, summon my son and kill him and only then kill Shivaji.” So Muhammad Amin Khan went to the Emperor and reported Ram Singh’s words. Whereupon the Emperor said: “Tell Ram Singh to stand surety for Shivaji. If Shivaji escapes or does any mischief Ram Singh will be responsible. Let Ram Singh give a security bond in writing.” When Muhammad Amin Khan returned and narrated this to Ram Singh, he agreed to give a security bond.

He returned to his camp and briefed Shivaji in the night of 14th May 1666. Shivaji came to Ram Singh the following morning, performed worship (puja) of Shiva there and gave his solemn assurance. Then Ram Singh wrote the security bond and gave it to Muhammad Amin Khan that afternoon in the antechamber (ghuslkhana). When the Khan reported this to the Emperor he took the bond and ordered: “Tell Ram Singh to take Shivaji to Kabul. Find an auspicious day to begin the journey.” Luckily, shivAjI visited and bribed Javar khAn to get pardoned for his “offences” and get that order cancelled.

Event occured on 1886-05-25 (gregorian).

Rash bihArI bose born. He was one of the key organisers of the Ghadar Mutiny, and later the Indian National Army (after escaping to Japan in 1915 when the mutiny failed). Rash Behari Bose handed over Indian National Army to Subhas Chandra Bose.

Hanuman Jayanti celebrated widely in Andhra Pradesh.

а§≠а§¶а•На§∞а§Ха§Ња§≥а•А-а§Ьа§ѓа§®а•На§§а•А

Observed on Krћ•бє£бєЗa-ƒТkƒБda≈ЫƒЂ tithi of Vai≈ЫƒБkhaбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

а§Ча•Ба§ѓа§Ња§®а§Њ-а§µа§ња§Єа•На§Ѓа§∞-а§єа§§а•На§ѓа§Ња§Г #а•Ђа•Ѓ

Event occured on 1964-05-26 (gregorian).

In a pre-planned attack on 3k Indians of Wismar, Guyana вАУ many Indians were murdered, many Indian men were beaten and brutalised, hundreds of Indian women were raped, many females were stripped naked, children were terrorised and traumatised, countless Indian homes and business were burned down, and with all fleeing Wismar for safety elsewhere.

PeopleвАЩs National Congress (PNC) held its party congress in Mackenzie, that adjoins Wismar, and celebrated the victory. Linden Forbes Burnham described May 26, 1964 as a day of victory because it was a day of defeat of Indians in Wismar-McKenzie, of running them out of the area. He renamed the place after himself, Linden. And he deliberately chose May 26 as the date for GuyanaвАЩs independence

  • Tags:
а§Ѓа•Ба§∞а§Ња§∞а•Н-а§ђа§Ња§Ьа•А-а§µа•Аа§∞-а§Ча§§а§ња§Г #а•©а•Ђа•≠

Event occured on 1665-05-26 (gregorian). Julian date was converted to Gregorian in this reckoning.

Mogols under Jai Singh, assisted by the paThAn Dilir Khan (with nephews Ghairat and Muzaffar), had beseiged closely situated purandara and rudramal forts. H mansabdArs of mogols like kirAta-simha, haribhAn kunwar, udaybhAn, chaturbhuj chauhAN, mitrasen, indraman bundela assisted. Daud Khan and Qutb ud Din khAn had also joined. With huge cannons named ‘Abdulla Khan’, ‘Fath Lashkar’ and ‘havelI’ taken up opposite summits, the beseigers had downed rudramal. Mogols mined and bombarded safed-burj and kAlA burj - the undermining of which would lead to capture of the lower fort.

Taking 700 select men murAr bAjI prabhu sallied out in a desperate melee and attacked dilIr khAn. 500 paThAns died as did 300 marAThas. murAr and few desperados dashed right up to dilIr khAn’s position. Refusing generous surrender promises, he ran up to dilIr khAn sword in hand. dilIr downed him with an arrow. The rest ran back to the fort to defend it to the death. (sabhAsad chronicle.)

а§Єа§∞а•На§µ-а§Еа§™а§∞а§Њ-а§Па§Ха§Ња§¶а§ґа•А

The Krishna-paksha Ekadashi of vai≈ЫƒБkha month is known as aparƒБ-ƒУkƒБda≈ЫƒЂ.

а§ґа§ђа§∞а•Аа§Ча§ња§∞а§њ-а§¶а•За§µа§Єа•На§•а§Ња§®-а§®а§Ња§ґа§Г #а•≠а•®

Event occured on 1950-05-26 (gregorian).

On this day, shabarImala temple was found desecrated. Police report (by DIG KK Menon) suspected Christians.

First noticed by a priest on June 14, 1950, the arson was reported to police two days later by the commissioner and the president of Travancore Devaswom Board. The temple was last closed on May 20. The priest, his subordinates and some worshippers had left the place, with nobody staying back. When Kollam district superintendent of police visited the place, he had noticed that, вАЬThe idol was found broken; the head, left hand palm and fingers separated. There were cut marks on the face of the idol as well as on the forehead.вАЭ The enquiry had found out that the fire was not an accident. There were 15 cut marks of violence on the door leading to the sreekovil (sanctum sanctorum), indicating forcible entry, the report said. Police had found traces of brass on the sharp edge of an axe found from the spot. Theft attempt was ruled out as silver, gold and utensils were not taken away.

Aftermath: The temple was rebuilt, the stone idol replaced with a metal one.

а§Еа§Ча•На§®а§ња§®а§Ха•На§Ја§§а•На§∞-а§Єа§Ѓа§Ња§™а§®а§Ѓа•Н

The end of the period agninakбє£atram, the transit of the Sun through krћ•ttikƒБ asterism.

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: SpecialPeriodEnd
а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•© а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§Ьа•На§Юа§Ња§§а•На§Ѓа•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•®а•©а•Ѓа•ђ

Observed on Krћ•бє£бєЗa-Caturda≈ЫƒЂ tithi of Vai≈ЫƒБkhaбЄ• (lunar) month (AparƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/vyaapti). The event occurred in 2738 (Kali era).

This Sarvaj√±ƒБtman, who was born to Vardhana got initiated into asceticism even before the age of seven by ≈Ъri ≈ЪaбєЕkara himself on being delighted to have had debate with him on the banks of river TƒБmraparбєЗi; he spent seventy years along with Sure≈Ыvara in that PƒЂбє≠ha and then lived in that Maбє≠ha for forty two years. The sage known as Sarvaj√±a attained union with his own Self by doing service at the lotus feet of ≈Ъri PadmapƒБda, who was dear to the ƒАcƒБrya (≈Ъri ≈ЪaбєЕkara) and imparting the unparallel doctrine through CinmudrƒБ to the saint by name Brahmasvar≈Ђpa who served with love and faith at the feet of the master who was holding the pontiff-dom of the DvƒБravatƒЂ maбє≠ha. The sage Sarvaj√±ƒБtman whose works such as SaбєБkбє£epa≈ЫƒБrƒЂraka showed clearly/elaborately the collective meaning of the words hidden in the great commentatorial works on Advaita merged in the Supreme on the fourteenth day of the dark fortnight in the month of Vai≈ЫƒБkha in the Nala year of the Kali era 2737 at KƒБ√±ci ≈Ъri ≈ЪaбєЕkara Maбє≠ha.

а§Йа§§а•На§Єа§µа§Ња§Г

  • а§Ха•Га§§а•На§§а§ња§Ха§Њ-а§µа•На§∞а§§а§Ѓа•Н, а§Ха•Га§Ја•На§£а§¶а•За§µа§∞а§Ња§ѓа§Єа•На§ѓ а§∞а§Ња§ѓа§Ъа•Ва§∞а•Б-а§Ьа§ѓа§Г #а•Ђа•¶а•®, а§™а§Юа•На§Ъ-а§™а§∞а•На§µ-а§™а•Ва§Ьа§Њ (а§Еа§Ѓа§Ња§µа§Ња§Єа•На§ѓа§Њ)
а§Ха•Га§Ја•На§£а§¶а•За§µа§∞а§Ња§ѓа§Єа•На§ѓ а§∞а§Ња§ѓа§Ъа•Ва§∞а•Б-а§Ьа§ѓа§Г #а•Ђа•¶а•®

Event occured on 1520-05-29 (gregorian). Julian date was converted to Gregorian in this reckoning.

Krishnadeva Raya of Vijayanagar routed the Bijapur army of Ismail Adil Shah which had come to save Raichur fort (beseiged since around March) and captured it.

Ismail hoped that his artillery would decimate Vijayanagara attackers - but Krishnadevaraya only sent a division under kumAra vijaya for a vigorous attack. As the bijApur vanguard retreated, the artillery opened fire forcing kumAra vijaya’s forces to retreat - pursued by bijApur forces. At that time, the rAya joined with the rest of his army crushing the pursuers. bijApur army fled - many drowned in the kRShNA river at their rear. Salabat Khan tried to stop the rout, but his band of 500 portuguese mercenaries were eliminated and Salabat captured as they almost reached the rAya’s guard. Adil Shah, who did not fight at all, ran away on an elephant with 500 horse. Horse trader Christovao de Figueiredo then offered services of his band of 20 - they picked off fort defenders (including the governor) using their arquebusses. After that the fort was captured.

Context: Vijayanagara had captured rAyachUru earlier in 1510, but they’d lost it in 1516 while busy fighting gajapati-s. Krishnadevaraya, aged 35, sent Seyed Maraikar, a Muslim in his service, to Goa with 40k gold coins to buy horses. Maraikar betrayed Krishnadevaraya’s cause and went to Ismail Adil Shah with the money and offered his services. Krishnadevaraya made a demand that Maraikar be returned along with the money. Ismail refused and instead helped him escape to Goa. During the period of peace Krishnadevaraya made extensive preparations for a grand attack on Raichur Doab. After the court decided that Raichur should be attacked, the king invited all commanders (Nayakas) in his service to take part in the battle.

Aftermath: Nizam Shah, Qutb Shah, Barid Shah and Imad Shah wrote to the rAya asking him to return rAyachUru or that they would combine against him. The rAya replied that they should not trouble themselves - he would visit them. Ismail dallied until the rAya marched on to bijApur itself (1523). Sadly, just 7 years later, aged 45, kRShNadevarAya died even as he planned to capture beLgaum.

  • Tags:
а§Ха•Га§§а•На§§а§ња§Ха§Њ-а§µа•На§∞а§§а§Ѓа•Н

Observed on every occurrence of Krћ•ttikƒБ nakshatra (S≈ЂryƒБstamayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

krћ•ttikƒБ-vratam for Lord Subrahmanya. Observe fast for the entire day, and obtain blessings of all the krћ•ttikƒБ-striyaбЄ• who raised skanda after His birth. Three Vratas are very special for Subrahmanya—the bhrћ•guvƒБra-subrahmaбєЗya-vratam in tulƒБmƒБsa, this regular krћ•ttikƒБ vratam, and skandaбє£aбє£бє≠hivratam.

а§™а§Ха•На§Ја§Ња§®а•На§§а§Њ а§Йа§™а§µа§Єа•На§§а§µа•На§ѓа§Ња§Г, а§™а§Ха•На§Ја§Ња§¶а§ѓа•Л а§ља§≠а§ња§ѓа§Ја•На§Яа§µа•На§ѓа§Ња§Га•§ а§Ха§ња§Юа•На§Ъ - ‘а§Еа§•а§Ња§™а§∞а§Ња§єа•На§£ а§Па§µа§Ња§™а•На§≤а•Ба§§а•На§ѓа•Ма§™а§µа§Єа§•а§ња§Ха§В а§¶а§Ѓа•На§™а§§а•А а§≠а•Ба§Юа•На§Ьа•Аа§ѓа§Ња§§а§Ња§В а§ѓа§¶а•Н а§Па§®а§ѓа•Ла§Г а§Ха§Ња§Ѓа•На§ѓа§В а§Єа•На§ѓа§Ња§§а•Н- а§Єа§∞а•На§™а§ња§∞а•Н а§Ѓа§ња§ґа•На§∞а§В а§Єа•На§ѓа§Ња§§а•Н а§Ха•Ба§ґа§≤а•За§®’а•§

‘а§Еа§ђа§єа•Ба§µа§Ња§¶а•А а§Єа•На§ѓа§Ња§§а•На•§ а§Єа§§а•На§ѓа§В а§µа§ња§µа§¶а§ња§Ја•За§§а•На•§ а§Еа§І а§Па§µа•Иа§§а§Ња§В а§∞а§Ња§§а•На§∞а§ња§В а§ґа§ѓа•Аа§ѓа§Ња§§а§Ња§Ѓа•На•§ а§§а•М а§Ца§≤а•Б а§Ьа§Ња§Ча•На§∞а§®а•Н-а§Ѓа§ња§ґа•На§∞а§Ња§µа•Н а§Па§µа•Иа§§а§Ња§В а§∞а§Ња§§а•На§∞а§ња§В а§µа§ња§єа§∞а•За§ѓа§Ња§§а§Ња§Ѓа•Н а§За§§а§ња§єа§Ња§Є-а§Ѓа§ња§ґа•На§∞а•За§£ а§µа§Њ а§Ха•За§®а§Ъа§ња§¶а•Н а§µа§Ња•§ а§Ьа•Ба§Ча•Ба§™а•На§Єа•За§ѓа§Ња§§а§Ња§В а§§а•На§µа•Н а§Па§µа§Ња§µа•На§∞а§§а•На§ѓа•За§≠а•На§ѓа§Г а§Ха§∞а•На§Ѓа§≠а•На§ѓа§Га•§’

а§™а§∞а•За§¶а•На§ѓа•Ба§∞а•Н а§єа•Ла§Ѓа§Ња§ѓ а§Єа§Ѓа•На§≠а§Ња§∞а§Єа§Ѓа•На§≠а§∞а§£а§Ња§¶а§ѓа•Л а§µа•На§ѓа§µа§Єа•На§•а§Ња§Г а§Ха§Ња§∞а•На§ѓа§Ња§Га•§

а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§ѓа§ґа•На§Ъа§ња§§а•На§§а§Ња§∞а•На§•а§Ѓа•Н а§Еа§™а§њ а§Ха§ња§Юа•На§Ъа§®а•Н а§µа•На§∞а§§а§Ѓа•Н

“а§™а§∞а•На§µа§£а§њ а§µа§Њ а§§а§ња§≤а§≠а§Ха•На§Ј а§Йа§™а•Ла§Ја•На§ѓ а§µа§Њ а§ґа•На§µа•Ла§≠а•Ва§§ а§Йа§¶а§Ха§Ѓа•Ба§™а§Єа•На§™а•Га§ґа•На§ѓ а§Єа§Ња§µа§ња§§а•На§∞а•Аа§В а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§ґа§Г а§Єа§єа§Єа•На§∞а§Ха•Га§§а•На§µ а§Жа§µа§∞а•На§§а§ѓа•За§¶а•Н а§Еа§™а•На§∞а§Ња§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§ґа•Л а§µа§Њ” а§За§§а•На§ѓа•Н а§Жа§™а§Єа•На§§а§Ѓа•На§ђа§Іа§∞а•На§Ѓа§Єа•Ва§§а•На§∞а•За§Ја•Ба•§

а§™а§ња§£а•На§°-а§™а§ња§§а•Г-а§ѓа§Ьа•На§Юа§Г

Pinda Pitru Yajnam is a ≈ЫrƒБddham to be done to two sets of Pitru Devatas. It is to be done in one’s Agnihotra Agni or if that is not present then Aupasana Agni. Rice is cooked in a section of the Agni moved to the south-east. From this, even those whose father is living, give ƒБhuti to the Deva Pitru-s ie s≈Нma, yama and agni kavyavƒБhana. Those whose father is not living should additionally give piбєЗбЄНas to the Manushya Pitru-s also to whom amƒБvƒБsyƒБ-≈ЫrƒБddham is also performed.

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: ShraddhaDays
а§Єа•Ла§Ѓа§µа§§а•А а§Еа§Ѓа§Ња§µа§Ња§Єа•На§ѓа§Њ

amƒБvƒБsyƒБ on a Monday is as sacred as a solar eclipse. Particularly good for performing pradakshinam of Pippala tree.

а§Еа§Ѓа§Ња§µа§Єа•На§ѓа§Њ а§§а•Б а§Єа•Ла§Ѓа•За§® а§Єа§™а•На§§а§Ѓа•А а§≠а§Ња§®а•Ба§®а§Њ а§Єа§єа•§

amƒБvƒБsyƒБ of the vai≈ЫƒБkha month.

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: AmavasyaDays

It is said in the Smrtis, that if Amavaysa falls on a Monday, Tuesday or Thursday, such a tithi is given the appellation puбє£kalƒБ and is as sacred as a solar eclipse.

а§¶а§∞а•На§ґ-а§Єа•На§•а§Ња§≤а•Аа§™а§Ња§Ха§Г

Observed on ≈Ъukla-PrathamƒБ tithi of every (lunar) month (P≈ЂrvƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/puurvaviddha). sthƒБlƒЂpakaбЄ• is an important fortnightly ritual, involving the offering of haviбє£yƒБnna to agni, with the offering being cooked on the fire itself.

а§Йа§§а•На§Єа§µа§Ња§Г

  • а§∞а§Ѓа•На§≠а§Њ-а§§а•Га§§а•Аа§ѓа§Њ, а§Єа§Ња§ѓа§®-а§µа•На§ѓа§§а•Аа§™а§Ња§§а§Г, а§єа§∞а§ња§Єа§ња§Ва§єа•За§®а§Ња§Яа§Х-а§Ча•На§∞а§єа§£а§Ѓа•Н #а•®а•¶а•™
а§єа§∞а§ња§Єа§ња§Ва§єа•За§®а§Ња§Яа§Х-а§Ча•На§∞а§єа§£а§Ѓа•Н #а•®а•¶а•™

Event occured on 1818-06-02 (gregorian).

On this day, Hari Singh Nalwa, Misr Diwan Chand and others at the head of about 25k khAlsa troops won Multan after a 3 month seige. Nawab Muzaffar Khan and sons were killed defending the fort.

Context

The Sikh Empire had attacked Multan multiple times in the past, the largest attack being in 1810. However, on the previous occasions the Sikh forces would defeat the defending force and seize the city only to have the governor of Multan, Muzaffar Khan Sadozai, retreat into the Multan Fort. During previous sieges, the Sikhs had settled for large single payments of tribute, while the attack in 1810 resulted in Multan paying a yearly tribute.

Aftermath

The siege of Multan ended significant Afghan influence in the Peshawar region and led to multiple Sikh attempts at capturing and the final capture of Peshawar.

Details

  • Tags:
а§∞а§Ѓа•На§≠а§Њ-а§§а•Га§§а•Аа§ѓа§Њ

Observed on ≈Ъukla-Trћ•tƒЂyƒБ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (PrƒБtaбЄ•/paraviddha).

Rambha Vratam; perform Savitri pooja near Banana tree. Offer naivedyam of bananas prepared with ghee.

Observed on ≈Ъukla-CaturthƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

Perform puja of Shiva-Parvati under a banana tree or on a banana leaf, do naivedyam of 100+ bananas and give away to small girl children.

а§™а•За§ґа•На§µа§Њ-а§∞а§Ња§Ьа•На§ѓ-а§®а§Ња§ґа§Г #а•®а•¶а•™

Event occured on 1818-06-04 (gregorian).

Peshwa Bajirao II surrendered to the British, after an eight month long war (The 3rd Anglo-Maratha War) - a war which began in Nov 1817 with the ‘Battle of Khadki’.

а§Ја§Ја•На§†а•А-а§µа•На§∞а§§а§Ѓа•Н

а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•Ђа•¶ а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§Ъа§®а•На§¶а•На§∞а§Ъа•Ва§°а•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а•® а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•≠а•®а•ђ

Observed on ≈Ъukla-бєҐaбє£бє≠hƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (AparƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/vyaapti). The event occurred in 4398 (Kali era).

Then the preceptor Chandrac≈ЂбЄНa, son of AruбєЗagiri, by name GaбєЕge≈Ыa (before initiation), governing the preceptorship for fifty years, He, avowed to the rigid worship of Lord ≈Ъiva, attained siddhi on the night of ≈Ъuklapakбє£aбє£aбє£бє≠hi of the month Jyeбє£бє≠a in the year Durmukhi. These two preceptorsвАФ≈ЪrƒЂ MahƒБdeva and ≈ЪrƒЂ JƒБhnavƒЂtirthas performed innumerable yƒБgas and attained siddhi on the banks of river GaruбЄНa (KaбЄНilam).

а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•ђ а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§ґа•Ба§¶а•На§Іа§Ња§®а§®а•На§¶а•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•®а•Іа•™а•ђ

Observed on ≈Ъukla-бєҐaбє£бє≠hƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (AparƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/vyaapti). The event occurred in 2978 (Kali era).

≈ЪrƒЂ Vi≈ЫvanƒБtha, son of Vaidyabharva of VedƒБraбєЗya, received initiation from sage J√±ƒБnƒБnanda, became ≈ЪuddhƒБnandamunƒЂ≈Ыvara was established in KƒБ√±cƒЂ; having carried on the responsibilities of the preceptor for eighty-one years, he secured union on the sixth day of the bright fortnight in the year Nala.

а§Єа•Ла§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§ња§Ѓа§Ња§∞ а§®а§Ња§ѓа§©а§Ња§∞а•Н (а•©а•®) а§Ча•Ба§∞а•Ба§™а•Ва§Ьа•И

Observed on ƒА≈ЫrƒУбє£ƒБ nakshatra of Vrћ•бє£abhaбЄ• (sidereal solar) month (PrƒБtaбЄ•/paraviddha).

а§µа§ња§®а•На§Іа•На§ѓа§Ња§µа§Ња§Єа§ња§®а•А-а§¶а•За§µа•А-а§™а•Ва§Ьа§Њ

Observed on ≈Ъukla-бєҐaбє£бє≠hƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: SpecialPuja
а§ґа•На§∞а•Аа§®а§ња§µа§Ња§Єа§Ѓа§Ща•На§Ча§Ња§™а•Ба§∞а•З а§Єа§Ња§Ха•На§Ја§Ња§§а•На§Ха§Ња§∞-а§µа•Иа§≠а§µа•Ла§§а•На§Єа§µа§Г

Observed on ≈Ъukla-бєҐaбє£бє≠hƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

According to historical evidences, dated 22nd March 1540 CE, the Temple was brought to worship by doing Jeernoddhaarana by Chinni Thirumalacharyulu, the grandson of great saint Sri Talapaka Annamacharyulu. In 1940 CE. an Archaka Swamulu by name Sundararaju from Tamilnadu came to Srinivasa Mangapuram. He shared his dream with villagers by saying that in his dream Lord Venkateswara Swamy appeared and asked him to bring the temple to Past Vaibhavam (glorious) by performing Deepa Naivedya Aaradhana and Lord disppeared. As per the Lord words, Archaka Swami Sundaraju performed first pooja on July 1940 which was coincided on Ashada Shudda Shasti Day with the help of village elders. To mark the auspicious day of revival of Nitya Pooja Kainkaryam, TTD has been conducting this festival as Sakshatkara Vaibhavotsavam on Ashada Shuddha Shasti Day in the Jyaishta month every year.

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: AndhraTempleFestivals

а§Йа§§а•На§Єа§µа§Ња§Г

  • а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•®а•© а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§Єа§Ъа•На§Ъа§ња§§а•На§Єа•Ба§Ца•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•Іа•Ђа•Іа•І, а§ґа•На§∞а•Аа§®а§ња§µа§Ња§Єа§Ѓа§Ща•На§Ча§Ња§™а•Ба§∞а•З а§Єа§Ња§Ха•На§Ја§Ња§§а•На§Ха§Ња§∞-а§µа•Иа§≠а§µа•Ла§§а•На§Єа§µ-а§Єа§Ѓа§Ња§™а§®а§Ѓа•Н
а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•®а•© а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§Єа§Ъа•На§Ъа§ња§§а•На§Єа•Ба§Ца•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•Іа•Ђа•Іа•І

Observed on ≈Ъukla-SaptamƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (AparƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/vyaapti). The event occurred in 3613 (Kali era).

Son of ≈ЪrƒЂ CikkƒБkula SomanƒБrya, named GirƒЂ≈Ыa earlier. initiated with the tile Saccitsukha by Parip≈ЂrбєЗabodha; an ardent devotee of Lord Subrahmanya, He made pious the astrologer ƒАryabhaбє≠бє≠a by making him carry out the expiatory rite for his long voyage (on sea). He, Saccitsukha, having instituted Citsukha in his place, attained siddhi on the seventh day of the bright fortnight of the month of VбєЫбє£abha in the year Khara.

а§Йа§§а•На§Єа§µа§Ња§Г

  • а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•Іа•ђ а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§Йа§Ьа•На§Ьа•На§µа§≤ а§ґа§Ща•На§Ха§∞а•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•Іа•ђа•Ђа•ђ, а§Іа•Ва§Ѓа§Ња§µа§§а•А-а§Ьа§ѓа§®а•На§§а•А, а§µа§∞а•Ба§£-а§™а•Ва§Ьа§Њ
а§Іа•Ва§Ѓа§Ња§µа§§а•А-а§Ьа§ѓа§®а•На§§а•А

Observed on ≈Ъukla-Aбє£бє≠amƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (MadhyarƒБtriбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

Goddess Dhumavati is 7th of the Dasha Maha Vidyas.

Details
  • References
    • http://hindupad.com/dhumavati-jayanti-maa-dhumavathi-jayanthi/
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: Dashamahavidya
а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•Іа•ђ а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§Йа§Ьа•На§Ьа•На§µа§≤ а§ґа§Ща•На§Ха§∞а•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•Іа•ђа•Ђа•ђ

Observed on ≈Ъukla-Aбє£бє≠amƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (AparƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/vyaapti). The event occurred in 3468 (Kali era).

Born on the banks of river Tapati as the son of ≈ЪaбєЕkarake≈Ыavasya and avowed to rigid celibacy ≈ЪrƒЂ Acyutake≈Ыava, known as Ujjvala≈ЫaбєЕkara, after being initiated by GƒЂбє£pati, patronised by Va√±cƒЂ≈Ыavara, uprooted the rival schools and with fame the great saint reached Kashmir. Then having spent thirty-eight years in the PƒЂбє≠ha of the Universal Preceptor, He merged in Brahman, the eternal free from joy and sorrow and the everlasting on the eighth day of the bright fortnight in VбєЫбє£a month of the year Akбє£aya in the Kali era 3468 in the place called KalƒБpur in Kashmir.

а§ђа•Ба§Іа§Ња§Ја•На§Яа§Ѓа•А

aбє£бє≠amƒЂ tithi on a Wednesday is as sacred as a solar eclipse.

а§Еа§Ѓа§Ња§µа§Єа•На§ѓа§Њ а§§а•Б а§Єа•Ла§Ѓа•За§® а§Єа§™а•На§§а§Ѓа•А а§≠а§Ња§®а•Ба§®а§Њ а§Єа§єа•§

а§Ъа§§а§Єа•На§∞а§Єа•На§§а§ња§•а§ѓа§Єа•На§§а•На§µа•За§§а§Ња§Г а§Єа•Ва§∞а•На§ѓа§Ча•На§∞а§єа§£а§Єа§®а•На§®а§ња§≠а§Ња§Га••

а§Ьа•На§ѓа•За§Ја•На§†а§Ња§Ја•На§Яа§Ѓа•А

Observed on ≈Ъukla-Aбє£бє≠amƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: LessCommonFestivals
а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•ђа•І а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§Ѓа§єа§Ња§¶а•За§µа•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а•™ а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•®а•≠а•≠

Observed on ≈Ъukla-NavamƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (AparƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/vyaapti). The event occurred in 4847 (Kali era).

Here (in this world) the best among preceptors by name MahƒБdevendra adorned the incomparable seat of preceptor for forty-two years; thus the great Yogi realised the Self and attained siddhi on the ninth day of bright fortnight in the month of Jyeбє£бє≠ha in the year Krodhana. ƒАtmabodha, who wrote a commentary Suбє£amƒБ on ≈ЪrƒЂ SadƒБ≈ЫivabrahmendraвАЩs GururatnamƒБlika also belonged to this period (a contemporary of this preceptor). Only during this period, the Jagadguru Maбє≠ha moved out of KƒБ√±ci. The place where this present preceptor attained siddhi was the wellknown Tiruvottiyur situated in the north of Madras (≈Ъalivahana era 1664).

а§Еа§¶а•На§µа•Иа§§а§Ња§§а•На§Ѓа§™а•На§∞а§Ха§Ња§ґа•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞а§Ња§¶а•Н а§ђа§Ња§≤а•На§ѓ а§Жа§ґа•На§∞а§ња§§а§Єа§Ва§ѓа§Ѓа§Га•§

а§Єа§Ѓа§Ња§ґа•На§Ъа§§а•На§µа§Ња§∞а§ња§Ва§ґа§¶а•Н а§¶а•На§µа§ња§ґа§∞а§¶а§Іа§ња§Ха§Њ а§ђа§ња§≠а•На§∞а§¶а•Н а§Еа§§а•Ба§≤а§Ња§Ѓа•На•§

а§ђа•На§∞а§єа•На§Ѓа§Ња§£а•А-а§™а•Ва§Ьа§Њ

Observed on ≈Ъukla-NavamƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

brahmƒБбєЗƒЂ is one of the seven mƒБtrћ•kƒБs

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: SpecialPuja
а§¶а•За§µа•А-а§™а§∞а•На§µ-а•©

Observed on ≈Ъukla-Da≈ЫamƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (ƒА≈ЫvinaбЄ•/paraviddha).

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: Devi Parva
а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•Іа•≠ а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§Єа§¶а§Ња§ґа§ња§µа•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•Іа•ђа•™а•Ѓ

Observed on ≈Ъukla-Da≈ЫamƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (AparƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/vyaapti). The event occurred in 3476 (Kali era).

The one who was born to a brahmin ≈ЪrƒЂdeva Mi≈ЫrƒБ; soon after the birth uttered the Truth that вАШeverything is ƒАtmanвАЩ due to the samskƒБras of the previous birth; the Buddhists and Jains unable to counter the doctrine threw him in the river Sindhu, who saved his life by placing him lovingly on the lotus petals. Being carried by her (river Sindhu) He (SadƒБ≈ЫivabƒБla) was given to a brahmin endowed with austerities saying вАЬpleased by your austerities/penance today this boy/child is givenвАЭ; then being asked/enquired by Bh≈Ђrivasu of Pushpapura and initiated by himвАФthe saviourвАФfather, He held high the school of nondualism. Preceptor ≈ЪrƒЂ SadƒБ≈ЫivabƒБla having served with reverence ≈ЪrƒЂ Ujjvala ≈ЪaбєЕkarƒБcƒБrya, got initiation, adhering to the principles of asceticism well carried out voyage in palanquin. Preceptor SadƒБ≈Ыiva, always intent on feeding one thousand brahmins, moved around everywhere imparted as per injunctions, for fifty times and governing the maбє≠ha of the Universal Preceptor for eight years, then installing Surendra spent sometime, reached the tranquil state in Tamaraparni (He attained jivasamadhi in Triyambakeshwar.). SadƒБ≈Ыiva, the great among ascetics, the personification of Lord ≈Ъiva, the accomplished One, attained his final beatitude on the tenth day of the bright fortnight in the month of Jyeбє£бє≠ha of the year Bhava.

вАФа§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§ґа•На§≤а•Ла§Ха§Ѓа§Юа•На§Ьа§∞а•А

а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•Ђа•© а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§Ња§®а§®а•На§¶ а§Єа§¶а§Ња§ґа§ња§µа•За§®а•На§¶а•На§∞ а§Єа§∞а§Єа•На§µа§§а•А а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•Ђа•®а•Ђ

Observed on ≈Ъukla-Da≈ЫamƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (AparƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/vyaapti). The event occurred in 4599 (Kali era).

This preceptor SadƒБ≈Ыivendra, son of NƒБganƒБtha of NƒБgƒБraбєЗya, having got initiation into asceticism from ≈ЪaбєЕkarƒБnanda, with the title P≈ЂrбєЗƒБnanda, adored by/revered by the King of Nepal, sanctifying all on earth with grace, He stayed at the KƒБmaPƒЂбє≠ha in KƒБбєЕci for eight-one years. The auspicious, revered, pure preceptor merged in the beatitude devoid of rebirth on the tenth day of the bright fortnight of Jyeбє£бє≠ha month in the year PiбєЕgala. This preceptor who went upto Nepal attained Siddhi in KƒБбєЕci itself.

а§Ѓа§єа•За§ґ-а§®а§µа§Ѓа•А

Observed on ≈Ъukla-NavamƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

а§Ѓа•Ба§£а•На§°-а§ђа•Аа§∞а•На§Є-а§Ѓа•Га§§а•На§ѓа•Ба§Г #а•Іа•®а•®

Event occured on 1900-06-09 (gregorian).

On this day Birsa Munda died in jail, a few days after his capture. In a short life of about 25 years, he had developed into an anti-British and anti-Missionary muNDa leader.

Early in life he threw off a 3 year old Christian conversion (with the name “Birsa David”) required for German Mission school admission; studied rAmAyaNa and mahAbhArata influenced by Anand pANDa (3 years association) and another vaiShNava monk (3 months association), then wearing a tilaka and a yajjnopavIta. 3 more years later, he began to lead his own hindu-tribal amalgam “Birsait panth” as “Dharti Aba”, focusing on purity, vegetarianism, the need to fight in “kalyug” etc.. He sang songs inspiring muNDa-s to down “rAvaN”.

Vyatipata Shraddha day.

а§Еа§≤а§∞а•На§Ѓа•За§≤а•На§Ѓа§Ща•На§Ча§Ња§™а•Ба§∞а•З а§™а•На§≤а§µа•Ла§§а•На§Єа§µ-а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§∞а§Ѓа•На§≠а§Г

Rukmini Satyabhama sahita Krishna are taken out on the plava.

  • Edit config file
  • Tags: AndhraTempleFestivals
а§¶а§ґа§єа§∞а§Њ/а§Ча§Ща•На§Ча§Ња§µа§§а§∞а§£а§Ѓа•Н/а§¶а§ґа§™а§Ња§™а§єа§∞а§Њ-а§¶а§ґа§Ѓа•А

Observed on ≈Ъukla-Da≈ЫamƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

Avataranam of Ganga Devi. Bathe in any river and offer arghyam вАФ destroys 10 types of sins (da≈ЫaharƒБ). Also known as Ganga Jayanti, Dashapaapa-hara-dashami, Wednesday/Hasta nakshatra makes it even more special (Valmiki Ramayana).

а§¶а§ґа§Ѓа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§ґа•Ба§Ха•На§≤а§™а§Ха•На§Ја•З а§§а•Б а§Ьа•На§ѓа•За§Ја•На§†а•З а§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•З а§Ха•Ба§Ьа•З а§¶а§ња§®а•За•§
а§Ча§Ща•На§Ча§Ња§ља§µа§§а•Аа§∞а•На§£а§Њ а§єа§Єа•На§§а§∞а•На§Ха•На§Ја•З а§Єа§∞а•На§µа§™а§Ња§™а§єа§∞а§Њ а§Єа•На§Ѓа•Га§§а§Ња••
а§ѓа§Ња§В а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа§ња§§а•Н а§Єа§∞а§ња§§а§В а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§™а•На§ѓ а§¶а§¶а•На§ѓа§Ња§¶а§∞а•На§Ша•На§ѓа§В а§ґа•Ба§≠а•Ла§¶а§Ха§Ѓа•На•§
а§Ѓа•Ба§Ъа•На§ѓа§§а•З а§¶а§ґа§≠а§ња§Г а§™а§Ња§™а•Иа§Г а§Є а§Ѓа§єа§Ња§™а§Ња§§а§Ха•Ла§™а§Ѓа•Иа§Га••

Details
  • References
    • Nirnaya Sindu, Valmiki Ramayanam
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: OtherJayantis CommonFestivals
а§∞а§Ша•Ба§®а§Ња§•а§∞а§Ња§µ-а§ђа§®а•На§Іа§®а§Ѓа•Н #а•©а•Ѓ

Event occured on 1984-06-10 (gregorian).

On this day, 1,461 Sikh soldiers of the Sikh regimental centre (1,050 of them raw recruits) at Ramgarh camp in the erstwhile Bihar state rebelled and murdered their commander, Brigadier RS Puri. The leaders of the mutiny divided the troops into two groups just outside of Banaras to avoid a rumoured roadblock.

Context

Many Sikh armymen were indoctrinated by Khalistanis. Fifteen percent of the Indian Army - including some of its most elite divisions - were composed of Sikhs. (But Sikhs were less than 2 percent of the population.) One of the bodies removed from Bhindranwale’s basement command was that retired Maj. Gen. Shuhbeg Singh. He dismissed one day before getting his retirement. After his dismissal he joined the Khalistan movement as a military adviser. A couple of Sikh bodyguards later killed the prime minister Indira Gandhi.

The mutiny was sparked by an army assault the previous week on the holiest shrine of the Sikhs, the Golden Temple. Incidentally, the two top generals in the chain of command, Lt Gen RS Dayal MVC and Maj Gen KS Brar were Sikhs with distinguished records for service and gallantry.

Aftermath

Two other senior officers and a number of soldiers were seriously wounded in heavy shooting at the camp that began before noon and continued until 4 pm. The soldiers joined other angry Sikhs in hijacking private buses and trucks at gunpoint.

One half was engaged by army artillery at Shakteshgarh railway station; those who managed to escape were rounded up by 21st Mechanised Infantry Regiment. The other half engaged with the artillery and troops of 20th Infantry Brigade, during which 35 soldiers (both sides) were killed. Sikh soldiers remaining behind at the camp raised a white flag of surrender when the reinforcements arrived.

Overall (including other incidents), about 5k Sikhs had mutinied. Many mutineers were court-martialed and dismissed. The Sikh Regiment’s 9th battalion was disbanded. Some were retained, but soldiers from those battalions were made to wear a black band around their turbans to signify they had once betrayed the country.

  • Tags:
а§∞а§Ша•Ба§®а§Ња§•а§∞а§Ња§µ-а§ђа§®а•На§Іа§®а§Ѓа•Н #а•®а•Ђа•™

Event occured on 1768-06-10 (gregorian).

On this day, peshvA mAdhav rAv waged a war against Raghunathrao, captured him and put him under house arrest at Shaniwar Wada along with his assistant Sakharam Bapu Bokil.

Context: Raghunathrao had marched to the north for expanding the empire, he failed to do so. Instead, he came back to Anandvalli and was again tempted to form an alliance with his generals and fight against Madhavrao. This time, however; Madhavrao was extremely agitated with his uncle’s repeated attempts to overthrow him.

а§Еа§єа§ња§≤а•На§ѓа§Њ-а§ђа§Ња§ѓа§њ-а§Ьа§®а•На§Ѓ #а•®а•ѓа•≠

Event occured on 1725-06-11 (gregorian). Julian date was converted to Gregorian in this reckoning.

Ahilyabai was born on May 31 1725 to Patil Mankoji Shinde. She was a widow who became Malwa’s greatest queen. Defended our country & personally led armies into battle. Never plundered anyone. Developed Malwa into a prosperous kingdom. Rebuilt temples destroyed by “peaceful” people. Built Dharmashalas at Tirthas. Patronized arts.

Sridevi Bhudevi sahita Sundararaja Swami are taken out on the plava.

Observed on ≈Ъukla-DvƒБda≈ЫƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

а§Ча§µа§Ња§Ѓа§ѓа§®-а§¶а•На§µа§Ња§¶а§ґа•А

Observed on ≈Ъukla-DvƒБda≈ЫƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (S≈Ђry≈НdayaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

Perform puja of Lord Trivikrama (Sahasranamarchana), do naivedyam of mangoes and offer annadanam to needy.

а§Еа§єа•Ла§∞а§Ња§§а•На§∞а•За§£ а§¶а•На§µа§Ња§¶а§ґа•На§ѓа§Ња§В а§Ьа•На§ѓа•За§Ја•На§†а•З а§Ѓа§Ња§Єа§њ а§§а•На§∞а§ња§µа§ња§Ха•На§∞а§Ѓа§Ѓа•На•§

а§єа§∞а§ња§µа§Ња§Єа§∞а§Г
  • вЖТ11:14

The first quarter of dvƒБda≈ЫƒЂ tithi is not suitable for breaking fast (pƒБraбєЗa), and is known as harivƒБsaraбЄ•. Break fast once harivƒБsaraбЄ• is over.

а§µа•Иа§¶а•На§ѓа§®а§Ња§•а§¶а•Аа§Ха•На§Ја§ња§§а•Аа§ѓа•З а§§а§ња§•а§ња§®а§ња§∞а•На§£а§ѓа§Ха§Ња§£а•На§°а•З а§µа§ња§Ја•На§£а•Ба§Іа§∞а•На§Ѓа•З—
а§Еа§Єа§Ѓа•На§≠а§Ња§Ја•На§ѓа§Ња§Ва§Єа•На§§а•Б а§Єа§Ѓа•На§≠а§Ња§Ја•На§ѓ а§§а•Ба§≤а§Єа•На§ѓа§§а§Єа§ња§Ха§Ња§¶а§≤а§Ѓа•На•§

а§Жа§Ѓа§≤а§Ха•На§ѓа§Ња§Г а§Ђа§≤а§В а§µа§Ња§™а§њ а§™а§Ња§∞а§£а•З а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§ґа•На§ѓ а§ґа•Ба§¶а•На§Іа•На§ѓа§§а§ња••

а§Ха§Ња§Юа•На§Ъа•А а•® а§Ьа§Ча§¶а•На§Ча•Ба§∞а•Б а§ґа•На§∞а•А-а§Єа•Ба§∞а•За§ґа•На§µа§∞а§Ња§Ъа§Ња§∞а•На§ѓ а§Жа§∞а§Ња§Іа§®а§Њ #а•®а•™а•®а•Ѓ

Observed on ≈Ъukla-DvƒБda≈ЫƒЂ tithi of Jyaiбє£бє≠haбЄ• (lunar) month (AparƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/vyaapti). The event occurred in 2696 (Kali era).

A native of GauбЄНade≈Ыa, born in Kashmir, pious, ≈Ъri Sure≈Ыvara, secured the knowledge of P≈Ђrva MƒЂmƒБбєБsa from KumƒБrila Bhaбє≠бє≠a, well-versed in Vedas, and avowed to house-holder’s life; he engaged in debate with ƒАcƒБrya and got initiated into asceticism. Then, attending on ƒАcƒБrya, he reached the highest level in yogic practices and by the directions of the ƒАcƒБrya, he lived for seventy years at the KƒБmapƒЂбє≠ha in KƒБ√±ci. Then, in the year 2695 of the Kali era, on the night of the twelfth day of the bright fortnight in the month of Jyeбє£бє≠ha of the Bhava year that revered Sure≈Ыa, having taken the world by surprise through his fame by traversing throughout the earth, united/merged with the Supreme Being in the path known as Laya obtained the state of beautiful form of ≈ЪivaliбєЕga verily in the presence of on-lookers.

Lord Malayappa rides on Garuda Vahanam in all four mada streets. This usually happens during Brahmotsavams, but since 2006, this has been happening on every p≈ЂrбєЗimƒБ.

а§™а§Ха•На§Ја§Ња§®а•На§§а§Њ а§Йа§™а§µа§Єа•На§§а§µа•На§ѓа§Ња§Г, а§™а§Ха•На§Ја§Ња§¶а§ѓа•Л а§ља§≠а§ња§ѓа§Ја•На§Яа§µа•На§ѓа§Ња§Га•§ а§Ха§ња§Юа•На§Ъ - ‘а§Еа§•а§Ња§™а§∞а§Ња§єа•На§£ а§Па§µа§Ња§™а•На§≤а•Ба§§а•На§ѓа•Ма§™а§µа§Єа§•а§ња§Ха§В а§¶а§Ѓа•На§™а§§а•А а§≠а•Ба§Юа•На§Ьа•Аа§ѓа§Ња§§а§Ња§В а§ѓа§¶а•Н а§Па§®а§ѓа•Ла§Г а§Ха§Ња§Ѓа•На§ѓа§В а§Єа•На§ѓа§Ња§§а•Н- а§Єа§∞а•На§™а§ња§∞а•Н а§Ѓа§ња§ґа•На§∞а§В а§Єа•На§ѓа§Ња§§а•Н а§Ха•Ба§ґа§≤а•За§®’а•§

‘а§Еа§ђа§єа•Ба§µа§Ња§¶а•А а§Єа•На§ѓа§Ња§§а•На•§ а§Єа§§а•На§ѓа§В а§µа§ња§µа§¶а§ња§Ја•За§§а•На•§ а§Еа§І а§Па§µа•Иа§§а§Ња§В а§∞а§Ња§§а•На§∞а§ња§В а§ґа§ѓа•Аа§ѓа§Ња§§а§Ња§Ѓа•На•§ а§§а•М а§Ца§≤а•Б а§Ьа§Ња§Ча•На§∞а§®а•Н-а§Ѓа§ња§ґа•На§∞а§Ња§µа•Н а§Па§µа•Иа§§а§Ња§В а§∞а§Ња§§а•На§∞а§ња§В а§µа§ња§єа§∞а•За§ѓа§Ња§§а§Ња§Ѓа•Н а§За§§а§ња§єа§Ња§Є-а§Ѓа§ња§ґа•На§∞а•За§£ а§µа§Њ а§Ха•За§®а§Ъа§ња§¶а•Н а§µа§Ња•§ а§Ьа•Ба§Ча•Ба§™а•На§Єа•За§ѓа§Ња§§а§Ња§В а§§а•На§µа•Н а§Па§µа§Ња§µа•На§∞а§§а•На§ѓа•За§≠а•На§ѓа§Г а§Ха§∞а•На§Ѓа§≠а•На§ѓа§Га•§’

а§™а§∞а•За§¶а•На§ѓа•Ба§∞а•Н а§єа•Ла§Ѓа§Ња§ѓ а§Єа§Ѓа•На§≠а§Ња§∞а§Єа§Ѓа•На§≠а§∞а§£а§Ња§¶а§ѓа•Л а§µа•На§ѓа§µа§Єа•На§•а§Ња§Г а§Ха§Ња§∞а•На§ѓа§Ња§Га•§

“а§™а§∞а•На§µа§£а§њ а§µа§Њ а§§а§ња§≤а§≠а§Ха•На§Ј а§Йа§™а•Ла§Ја•На§ѓ а§µа§Њ а§ґа•На§µа•Ла§≠а•Ва§§ а§Йа§¶а§Ха§Ѓа•Ба§™а§Єа•На§™а•Га§ґа•На§ѓ а§Єа§Ња§µа§ња§§а•На§∞а•Аа§В а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§ґа§Г а§Єа§єа§Єа•На§∞а§Ха•Га§§а•На§µ а§Жа§µа§∞а•На§§а§ѓа•За§¶а•Н а§Еа§™а•На§∞а§Ња§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§ґа•Л а§µа§Њ” а§За§§а•На§ѓа•Н а§Жа§™а§Єа•На§§а§Ѓа•На§ђа§Іа§∞а•На§Ѓа§Єа•Ва§§а•На§∞а•За§Ја•Ба•§

а§§а§≤а•На§µа§Ња§∞-а§ґа§ња§Ца§∞-а§Ьа§ѓа§Г #а•®а•©

Event occured on 1999-06-14 (gregorian).

On this day, during the kArgil war, Major Manoj Talwar died capturing point 5765 (19k feet high) from pAkistAni jihAdi-s.

  • He was the son of Capt PL Talwar, a civil engineer who participated in the 1962 China war and helped build the laDhAk airport.
  • He couldвАЩve easily been an Army Doc but he chose NDA over AFMC.
  • It wasnвАЩt his turn to get posted at Siachen but he chose to get posted there and was granted an вАШout of turn postingвАЩ.
Events

Although the team was supposed to be led by a Junior Officer, Major Manoj volunteered to lead the mission. The Pakistani army was constantly firing from above. He ordered his team to go around the peak and flank the entrenched enemy from front. During their advance, he suffered from several splinter injuries by enemyвАЩs mortars and grenades but despite all the difficulties, he displayed rare determination, exemplary sense of courage and devotion to duty and kept pushing forward. Moments after he had finally captured the peak & had embedded the Indian Flag at point 5765 on the midnight of 13th June, a splinter from a mortar shelling hit his eye and he was gravely injured. He fell 19,000 feet below into the valley and the search for his body went on for hours till the early morning of 14th June 1999.

Aftermath
  • Point 5765 was named вАШTalwar TopвАЩ & the bunker вАШTalwar BunkerвАЩ.
  • On 17th of June, as his body was cremated.
  • All the markets, cinema halls and other public places were closed. The women in the houses did not cook food that day. He was respected and loved as the вАШSon Of Meerut.вАЩ
  • His parents received his last letter which he had written on 10th of June while sitting on his bunker.
  • Later in the year 2003, a statue was built in Meerut in his honour and was unveiled by Sh Rajnath Singh.

  • 11:44вЖТ18:43

Mithuna-Ravi-SaбєЕkramaбєЗa-бєҐaбЄНa≈ЫƒЂti Punyakala. Perform danam of clothes, food and water.

Observed on Krћ•бє£бєЗa-PrathamƒБ tithi of every (lunar) month (P≈ЂrvƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

‘а§™а§∞а•На§µа§£а§њ а§µа§Њ а§§а§ња§≤а§≠а§Ха•На§Ј а§Йа§™а•Ла§Ја•На§ѓ а§µа§Њ а§ґа•На§µа•Ла§≠а•Ва§§ а§Йа§¶а§Ха§Ѓа•Ба§™а§Єа•На§™а•Га§ґа•На§ѓ а§Єа§Ња§µа§ња§§а•На§∞а•Аа§В а§™а•На§∞а§Ња§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§ґа§Г а§Єа§єа§Єа•На§∞а§Ха•Га§§а•На§µ а§Жа§µа§∞а•На§§а§ѓа•За§¶а•Н а§Еа§™а•На§∞а§Ња§£а§Ња§ѓа§Ња§Ѓа§ґа•Л а§µа§Њ ' а§За§§а•На§ѓа•Н а§Жа§™а§Єа•На§§а§Ѓа•На§ђа§Іа§∞а•На§Ѓа§Єа•Ва§§а•На§∞а•За§Ја•Ба•§

а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£а§Ѓа§Ња§Єа•За§Ја•На§Яа§ња§Г

Observed on Krћ•бє£бєЗa-PrathamƒБ tithi of every (lunar) month (P≈ЂrvƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/puurvaviddha).

  • Edit config file

а§∞а§µа§њ-а§Єа§Ща•На§Ха•На§∞а§Ѓа§£-а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§Ха§Ња§≤а§Г
  • 05:57вЖТ18:08

16 ghatikas on either side of the transition of the Sun are sacred for various observances. In addition to specific puбєЗyakƒБlas like бє£aбЄНa≈ЫƒЂti, viбє£бєЗupadƒЂ etc., this is also useful, especially in cases where the other puбєЗyakƒБlas are inconvenient for appropriate performance of rituals such as snƒБna, dƒБna, tarpaбєЗa, etc.

а§Єа§Ња§ѓа§®-а§µа•Иа§Іа•Га§§а§ња§Г
  • вЖТ12:52

When the sum of the longitudes of the Moon and Sun is 360, it is sƒБyana-vaidhrћ•tiбЄ•, and a y≈НgaбЄ• that is to be excluded for auspicious activities.

Details
  • Edit config file
  • Tags: Combinations
а§Єа§Ща•На§Ха•На§∞а§Ѓа§£-а§¶а§ња§®-а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§µа§Ња§єа•На§£-а§™а•Ба§£а•На§ѓа§Ха§Ња§≤а§Г
  • 05:57вЖТ12:20

When the transit of the Sun (saбєЕkrƒБnti) happens in the first half of the day (p≈ЂrvƒБhбєЗa) or after midnight of the previous day, the first half of the day is a puбєЗyakƒБla for various observances. In addition to 16 ghatikas on either side of the transition, and specific puбєЗyakƒБlas like бє£aбЄНa≈ЫƒЂti, viбє£бєЗupadƒЂ etc., this is also useful, especially in cases where the other puбєЗyakƒБlas are inconvenient for appropriate performance of rituals such as snƒБna, dƒБna, tarpaбєЗa, etc.

  • Edit config file
  • Tags: SunSankranti CommonFestivals
а§ґа§ња§µа§∞а§Ња§Ьа•Л а§∞а§Ња§Ѓа§Єа§ња§Ва§єа•За§® а§∞а§Ха•На§Ја§ња§§а§Г а•® #а•©а•Ђа•ђ

Event occured on 1666-06-15 (gregorian). Julian date was converted to Gregorian in this reckoning.

shivAjI was under strict house arrest since 25th May after his petition to return home in exchange for 20 million Rs was refused. Quote below is from Mehendale’s book.

“Soon afterwards, probably on 5th June, it was reported to the Emperor that Shivaji’s and Ram Singh’s men were daily coming to Agra. The report was probably false. But it was enough to infuriate the Emperor. He ordered Siddi Fulad and the men of the artillery: “Go and seize Shivaji and kill him.” But it would seem that by this time Shivaji had won over not only Jafar Khan and Muhammad Amin Khan but the Emperor’s elder sister Jahan Ara as well, for she at once urged the Emperor: “Mirza Raja [Jai Singh] is a sincere servant of Your Majesty. Shivaji came here relying upon his assurance. If you kill him now no one will have any faith in your word.” It was due to her intervention that the Emperor rescinded the order. It was probably this incident which led Ram Singh to take additional precautions for Shivaji’s safety. Since 5th June, Ram Singh’s men began keeping watch around Shivaji inside the circle of guards which had been posted by the Emperor’s orders. Ram Singh justified this saying that the Emperor had kept Shivaji in his custody and he would be held responsible if Shivaji escaped or committed suicide.”

jayasiMha too helped: “After the incident of 12th May, the Emperor had written to Jai Singh informing him of what had occurred and had asked for his advice in the matter. To this, according to the Alamgirnama, Jai Singh had replied that as he had concluded a treaty and covenant with Shivaji, he would be obliged if Shivaji’s offences would be pardoned and that such a policy would also be advantageous in view of the campaign against Bijapur. He also instructed Ram Singh to ensure that Shivaji remained safe and the sanctity of his and Ram Singh’s promises to Shivaji remained inviolate. At first Jai Singh had even beseeched the Emperor to let Shivaji return home. "

Details

  • Tags:
а§™а•Ва§∞а•На§£-а§Єа•На§•а§Ња§≤а•Аа§™а§Ња§Ха§Г

Observed on Krћ•бє£бєЗa-PrathamƒБ tithi of every (lunar) month (P≈ЂrvƒБhбєЗaбЄ•/puurvaviddha). sthƒБlƒЂpakaбЄ• is an important fortnightly ritual, involving the offering of haviбє£yƒБnna to agni, with the offering being cooked on the fire itself.
